0001: #!/usr/bin/perl -w
0002: #!/usr/bin/perl -d:ptkdb
0003: #
0004: 
0005: use strict;
0006: 
0007: 
0008: my $loaded_mail_sender = eval "require Mail::Sender";
0009: 
0010: use Data::Transformator;
0011: 
0012: use Expect;
0013: 
0014: use Getopt::Long;
0015: 
0016: use Neurospaces::Tester;;
0017: 
0018: use YAML;
0019: 
0020: 
0021: my $option_check_test_names;
0022: my $option_dump_json;
0023: my $option_dump_perl;
0024: my $option_dump_yaml;
0025: my $option_email = defined $ENV{NEUROSPACES_HARNESS_OPTION_EMAIL} ? $ENV{NEUROSPACES_HARNESS_OPTION_EMAIL} : 0 ;
0026: my $option_flattenout;
0027: my $option_help;
0028: my $option_numerical_compare;
0029: my $option_output;
0030: my $option_output_figures;
0031: my $option_output_html;
0032: my $option_output_latex;
0033: my $option_output_levels = [];
0034: my $option_output_pdf;
0035: my $option_random_order;
0036: my $option_regex_selector = ".*";
0037: my $option_report_disabled;
0038: my $option_show;
0039: my $option_show_command_tests;
0040: my $option_tags = [];
0041: my $option_timeout_multiplier = 1;
0042: my $option_timings;
0043: my $option_trace;
0044: my $option_verbose;
0045: 
0046: 
0047: our $global_config;
0048: 
0049: my $global_all_output_levels
0050:     = [
0051:        "meta",
0052:        "module",
0053:        "command_definition",
0054:        "command_test",
0055:       ];
0056: 
0057: my $global_error_count = 0;
0058: my $global_fd_output;
0059: my $global_previous_command_side_effects;
0060: my $global_test_report;
0061: 
0062: 
0063: $SIG{'__DIE__'}
0064:     = sub
0065:       {
0066:           use Carp;
0067: 
0068:           print STDERR Carp::longmess(@_);
0069: 
0070:           $global_test_report->{global}->{status} = 'Died';
0071: 
0072:           report_exit(3, @_);
0073:       };
0074: 
0075: 
0076: $SIG{'INT'}
0077:     = sub
0078:       {
0079:           $global_test_report->{global}->{status} = 'Interrupted';
0080: 
0081:           report_exit(2);
0082:       };
0083: 
0084: 
0085: sub initialize_config_environment
0086: {
0087:     my $fd_initialization;
0088: 
0089:     open($fd_initialization, ">&STDOUT");
0090: 
0091:     my $environment_settings = $global_config->{environment_settings};
0092: 
0093:     foreach my $environment_setting_name (keys %$environment_settings)
0094:     {
0095:         my $environment_setting = $environment_settings->{$environment_setting_name};
0096: 
0097:         # if simply setting values in the environment
0098: 
0099:         if (!ref $environment_setting)
0100:         {
0101:             # set the value in the environment
0102: 
0103:             $ENV{$environment_setting_name} = $environment_setting;
0104:         }
0105: 
0106:         # if additional initialization required
0107: 
0108:         elsif (ref $environment_setting eq 'HASH')
0109:         {
0110:             # get environment_setting value
0111: 
0112:             my $value = $environment_setting->{value};
0113: 
0114:             # set the value in the environment
0115: 
0116:             $ENV{$environment_setting_name} = $value;
0117: 
0118:             # give feedback
0119: 
0120:             my $description = $environment_setting->{description};
0121: 
0122:             $description =~ s/%value/$value/g;
0123: 
0124:             if ($option_verbose)
0125:             {
0126:                 print $fd_initialization $description;
0127:             }
0128: 
0129:             # loop over shell commands for initialization
0130: 
0131:             my $shell_commands = $environment_setting->{initialization}->{shell};
0132: 
0133:             foreach my $shell_command (@$shell_commands)
0134:             {
0135:                 # replace value
0136: 
0137:                 $shell_command =~ s/%value/$value/g;
0138: 
0139:                 # execute command
0140: 
0141:                 if ($option_verbose)
0142:                 {
0143:                     print $fd_initialization "$0: Executing ($shell_command)\n";
0144:                 }
0145: 
0146:                 system $shell_command;
0147:             }
0148:         }
0149:         else
0150:         {
0151:             die "$0: illegal environment_settings, was processing $environment_setting";
0152:         }
0153:     }
0154: }
0155: 
0156: 
0157: sub initialize_globals
0158: {
0159:     $global_test_report
0160:         = {
0161:            %$global_test_report,
0162:            description => {
0163:                            command => $0,
0164:                            name => "Test report",
0165:                            package => $global_config->{package},
0166:                           },
0167:            global => {
0168:                       config => $global_config,
0169:                       error_count => $global_error_count,
0170:                       status => 'initializing',
0171:                       test_counters => {
0172:                                         module_definition => 0,
0173:                                         command_definition => 0,
0174:                                         command_test => 0,
0175:                                        },
0176:                       time_start => `date`,
0177:                      },
0178:            target => {
0179:                       OS => $^O,
0180:                       system => {
0181:                                  libc => (join '', `(ls -l /lib/libc-* && ls -l /usr/lib/libc-*) 2>/dev/null`),
0182:                                  uname => (join '', `uname -a`),
0183:                                  cpu => (join '', `cat /proc/cpuinfo`),
0184:                                 },
0185:                       packages => {
0186:                                    '/etc/lsb-release' => (join '', `cat /etc/lsb-release`),
0187:                                    autoconf => (join '', `autoconf --version`),
0188:                                    automake => (join '', `automake --version`),
0189:                                    bison => (join '', `bison --version`),
0190:                                    flex => (join '', `flex --version`),
0191:                                    gcc => (join '', `gcc 2>&1 --version && gcc 2>&1 -v`),
0192:                                    perl => (join '', `perl 2>&1 -v && perl 2>&1 -V`),
0193:                                    python => (join '', `python 2>&1 --version`),
0194:                                    python_installation => (join '', `./contrib/pythontest 2>&1`),
0195:                                    swig => (join '', `swig 2>&1 -version`),
0196:                                   },
0197:                      },
0198:           };
0199: }
0200: 
0201: 
0202: sub initialize_config
0203: {
0204:     # find the package core directory
0205: 
0206:     $global_config = do './tests.config';
0207: 
0208:     if (!defined $global_config)
0209:     {
0210:         die "No test configuration found";
0211:     }
0212: 
0213:     # add to tests directory to include paths
0214: 
0215:     if (!exists $global_config->{tests_directory})
0216:     {
0217:         $global_config->{tests_directory} = "./tests/specifications";
0218: 
0219:         $global_config->{introduction_filename} = "tests/introduction.html";
0220:     }
0221: 
0222:     unshift @INC, $global_config->{tests_directory};
0223: }
0224: 
0225: 
0226: sub main
0227: {
0228:     parse_options();
0229: 
0230:     initialize_config();
0231: 
0232:     initialize_config_environment();
0233: 
0234:     initialize_globals();
0235: 
0236:     open($global_fd_output, ">&STDOUT");
0237: 
0238:     # find all the test module files
0239: 
0240:     my $test_module_references = Heterarch::Test::Library::construct();
0241: 
0242:     # read the test module files
0243: 
0244:     my $test_module_library_contents = Heterarch::Test::Library::expand($global_fd_output, $test_module_references);
0245: 
0246:     my $test_modules = $test_module_library_contents->{test_modules};
0247: 
0248:     # compute the model library checksum
0249: 
0250:     my $library_sha_before;
0251: 
0252:     if (defined ($global_config->{model_library}))
0253:     {
0254:         $library_sha_before = ModelLibrary::sha();
0255:     }
0256: 
0257:     # the previous command had no side effects on the loaded model (since there is no model yet)
0258: 
0259:     $global_previous_command_side_effects = 0;
0260: 
0261:     # set status: running
0262: 
0263:     $global_test_report->{global}->{status} = 'Running';
0264: 
0265:     # randomize test module order if requested
0266: 
0267:     if (defined $option_random_order)
0268:     {
0269:         if ($option_verbose)
0270:         {
0271:             print $global_fd_output "$0: Randomizing test specifications, random seed is $option_random_order\n";
0272:         }
0273: 
0274:         # by default the randomizer will choose a random_seed
0275: 
0276:         my $random_seed;
0277: 
0278:         if ($option_random_order =~ /^[0-9]*$/
0279:             and $option_random_order ne 1)
0280:         {
0281:             $random_seed = $option_random_order
0282:         }
0283: 
0284:         # randomize the test module library
0285: 
0286:         ( $test_modules, $random_seed ) = Heterarch::Test::Library::randomize_order($test_modules, $random_seed);
0287: 
0288:         $global_test_report->{random_seed} = $random_seed;
0289:     }
0290:     else
0291:     {
0292:         $global_test_report->{random_seed} = 'not randomized';
0293:     }
0294: 
0295:     $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}
0296:         = {
0297:            module_definition => 0,
0298:            command_definition => 0,
0299:            command_test => 0,
0300:           };
0301: 
0302:     # create an execution context for running the tests
0303: 
0304:     my $execution_context = Heterarch::Test::ExecutionContext->new($global_fd_output, { library_sha_before => $library_sha_before, }, );
0305: 
0306:     # create a test executor for running the tests following the requested type of output
0307: 
0308:     my $executor = $option_output->new($execution_context);
0309: 
0310:     # loop over all module definitions
0311: 
0312:     foreach my $module_definition (@$test_modules)
0313:     {
0314:         bless $module_definition, "Heterarch::Test::Module";
0315: 
0316:         my $fd_module_output = $global_fd_output;
0317: 
0318:         my $error = $module_definition->md_run($execution_context, $executor, $fd_module_output);
0319: 
0320:         # increment module definition test count
0321: 
0322:         $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{module_definition}++;
0323:     }
0324: 
0325:     $global_test_report->{global}->{time_end} = `date`;
0326: 
0327:     # do final processing of the executor
0328: 
0329:     $executor->ex_terminate($global_fd_output, $test_module_library_contents);
0330: 
0331:     # end the execution_context
0332: 
0333:     $execution_context->terminate();
0334: 
0335:     # close pending commands
0336: 
0337:     Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::Interactive::close_pending();
0338: 
0339:     # close the test report
0340: 
0341:     $global_test_report->{global}->{status} = 'Finished';
0342: 
0343:     # final report
0344: 
0345:     report_exit(0, $test_module_library_contents);
0346: }
0347: 
0348: 
0349: sub parse_options
0350: {
0351:     $option_numerical_compare = $global_config->{numerical_compare} || 0;
0352: 
0353:     my $result
0354:         = GetOptions
0355:             (
0356:              "check-test-names!" => \$option_check_test_names,
0357:              "dump-json!" => \$option_dump_json,
0358:              "dump-perl!" => \$option_dump_perl,
0359:              "dump-yaml!" => \$option_dump_yaml,
0360:              "email" => \$option_email,
0361:              "flattenout" => \$option_flattenout,
0362:              "help!" => \$option_help,
0363:              "numerical-compare!" => \$option_numerical_compare,
0364:              "output=s" => \$option_output,
0365:              "output-figures" => \$option_output_figures,
0366:              "output-html" => \$option_output_html,
0367:              "output-latex" => \$option_output_latex,
0368:              "output-levels=s" => $option_output_levels,
0369:              "output-pdf" => \$option_output_pdf,
0370:              "randomize-order=s" => \$option_random_order,
0371:              "regex-selector=s" => \$option_regex_selector,
0372:              "report-disabled!" => \$option_report_disabled,
0373:              "show!" => \$option_show,
0374:              "show-command-tests!" => \$option_show_command_tests,
0375:              "tags=s" => $option_tags,
0376:              "timeout-multiplier=s" => \$option_timeout_multiplier,
0377:              "timings!" => \$option_timings,
0378:              "trace!" => \$option_trace,
0379:              "v|verbose+" => \$option_verbose,
0380:             );
0381: 
0382:     if (!$result)
0383:     {
0384:         die "$0: Error in option processing";
0385:     }
0386: 
0387:     if (not defined $option_output)
0388:     {
0389:         if ($option_output_html)
0390:         {
0391:             $global_test_report->{output_format} = 'yaml_exit';
0392: 
0393:             $option_output = "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::HTMLTable";
0394:         }
0395:         elsif ($option_output_latex)
0396:         {
0397:             $global_test_report->{output_format} = 'latex';
0398: 
0399:             $option_output = "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Latex";
0400:         }
0401:         elsif ($option_output_pdf)
0402:         {
0403:             $global_test_report->{output_format} = 'pdf';
0404: 
0405:             $option_output = "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::PDF";
0406:         }
0407:         elsif ($option_show)
0408:         {
0409:             $global_test_report->{output_format} = 'yaml_exit';
0410: 
0411:             $option_output = "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::YAML::Summary";
0412:         }
0413:         elsif ($option_show_command_tests)
0414:         {
0415:             $global_test_report->{output_format} = 'yaml_exit';
0416: 
0417:             $option_output = "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::YAML::CommandTests";
0418:         }
0419:         else
0420:         {
0421:             $global_test_report->{output_format} = 'executor';
0422: 
0423:             $option_output = "Heterarch::Test::Executor";
0424:         }
0425:     }
0426: 
0427:     if (not scalar @$option_output_levels)
0428:     {
0429:         use Clone 'clone';
0430: 
0431:         $option_output_levels = clone($global_all_output_levels);
0432:     }
0433: 
0434:     $global_test_report->{options}
0435:         = {
0436:            check_test_names => $option_check_test_names,
0437:            dump_json => $option_dump_json,
0438:            dump_perl => $option_dump_perl,
0439:            dump_yaml => $option_dump_yaml,
0440:            email => $option_email,
0441:            flattenout => $option_flattenout,
0442:            help => $option_help,
0443:            numerical_compare => $option_numerical_compare,
0444:            output => $option_output,
0445:            output_figures => $option_output_figures,
0446:            output_html => $option_output_html,
0447:            output_latex => $option_output_latex,
0448:            output_levels => $option_output_levels,
0449:            output_pdf => $option_output_pdf,
0450:            random_order => $option_random_order,
0451:            regex_selector => $option_regex_selector,
0452:            report_disabled => $option_report_disabled,
0453:            show => $option_show,
0454:            show_command_tests => $option_show_command_tests,
0455:            tags => $option_tags,
0456:            timeout_multiplier => $option_timeout_multiplier,
0457:            timings => $option_timings,
0458:            trace => $option_trace,
0459:            verbose => $option_verbose,
0460:           };
0461: 
0462:     if ($option_help)
0463:     {
0464:         print
0465:             "
0466: $0: test definition executor
0467: 
0468: options:
0469:     --check-test-names   check the pathnames of the specification files against the names of the tests.
0470:     --dump-json          dump test specifications to json files in /tmp/.
0471:     --dump-perl          dump test specifications to perl files in /tmp/.
0472:     --dump-yaml          dump test specifications to yaml files in /tmp/.
0473:     --email              allow to send emails, the default is taken from \$ENV{NEUROSPACES_HARNESS_OPTION_EMAIL}.
0474:     --flattenout         flattenout the test definitions before testing,
0475:                          this experimental feature might increase test performance,
0476:                          by recycling test definitions.
0477:     --help               print usage information.
0478:     --numerical-compare  attempt to compare numbers numerically when string differences are found (default enabled).
0479:     --output             the class that should generate the output (default is '$option_output').
0480:     --output-figures     generate figures for test output with a figure clause.
0481:     --output-html        generate HTML output.
0482:     --output-latex       generate Latex output.
0483:     --output-levels      generate output for these levels (default is '" . ( join ', ', @$global_all_output_levels ) . "'.
0484:     --output-pdf         generate PDF output.
0485:     --randomize-order    randomize the order of the tests before executing them (require List::Util to be installed).
0486:     --regex-selector     defines a regex to run specific tests.
0487:     --report-disabled    include information of disabled tests in the test report.
0488:     --show               show tests that would be run using the current configuration.
0489:     --show-command-tests show all the command definitions that would be run during test execution, including the input to those commands.
0490:     --tags               only test test modules that have been tagged with these tags (default: all tags).
0491:     --timeout-multiplier multiply all timeout values with this constant.
0492:     --timings            add timing information about the tests to the report.
0493:     --trace              enable tracing using the strace unix shell command.
0494:     --verbose            set verbosity level.
0495: ";
0496: 
0497:         exit 1;
0498:     }
0499: 
0500: }
0501: 
0502: 
0503: sub report_exit
0504: {
0505:     my $exit_code = shift;
0506: 
0507:     my $description = shift;
0508: 
0509:     if (defined $description
0510:         && $exit_code eq 3)
0511:     {
0512:         print STDERR "*** die: $description\n";
0513:     }
0514: 
0515:     # write the full report to a file
0516: 
0517:     my $report_filename = ">/tmp/report_$global_config->{package}->{name}.yml";
0518: 
0519:     eval
0520:     {
0521:         YAML::DumpFile($report_filename, $global_test_report);
0522:     };
0523: 
0524:     if ($@)
0525:     {
0526:         print STDERR "*** Error: Failed to write output report to $report_filename\n";
0527:     }
0528:     else
0529:     {
0530:         print $global_fd_output "*** Info: See '$report_filename' for the detailed test report\n";
0531:     }
0532: 
0533:     # if email enabled by the options
0534: 
0535:     if ($option_email)
0536:     {
0537:         # check for a default route
0538: 
0539:         #! from perl/basic.t
0540: 
0541:         my $no_default_route = (`/sbin/route` =~ /default/ ? '' : 'no default route to the internet found');
0542: 
0543:         if (!$no_default_route)
0544:         {
0545:             # ask if sending an email is ok
0546: 
0547:             my $default_answer_prompt = $global_error_count ? "Y/n" : "y/N";
0548: 
0549:             print $global_fd_output "\n---\n  These tests generated $global_error_count error(s)";
0550:             print $global_fd_output ( $global_error_count ? "  Because errors were found, you should really consider sending an email to inform the developer of this software package\n" : "" );
0551:             print $global_fd_output "\n  Ok to send an email to hugo.cornelis\@gmail.com for this test report,\n  this email will not reveal your identity to the recipient ?  [$default_answer_prompt]";
0552: 
0553:             my $answer = readline(*STDIN);
0554: 
0555:             # send an email if ok
0556: 
0557:             if ($answer =~ /^y/)
0558:             {
0559:                 #t perhaps should consider Mail::Builder, don't know
0560: 
0561:                 if ($loaded_mail_sender)
0562:                 {
0563:                     my $sender
0564:                         = Mail::Sender->new
0565:                             (
0566:                              {
0567: #                             smtp => 'googlemail.l.google.com',
0568:                               smtp => 'mta1.uthscsa.edu',
0569:                               from => 'hugo.cornelis@gmail.com',
0570:                              },
0571:                             );
0572: 
0573:                     print $global_fd_output "Sending ... should not take more than 30 seconds
0574: ";
0575: 
0576:                     my $message
0577:                         = (
0578:                            "neurospaces_harness test report\n"
0579:                            . "Generated on " . `date` . "\n"
0580:                            . "\n========\n"
0581:                            . Dump($global_test_report)
0582:                            . "\n========\n"
0583:                           );
0584: 
0585:                     $sender->MailMsg
0586:                         (
0587:                          {
0588:                           to => 'cornelis@uthscsa.edu',
0589:                           subject => '[neurospaces_harness] test report',
0590:                           msg => $message,
0591:                          },
0592:                         );
0593: 
0594:                     #       $sender->Attach
0595:                     #           (
0596:                     #            {
0597:                     #             description => 'global_test_report',
0598:                     #            },
0599:                     #           );
0600: 
0601:                     $sender->Close();
0602: 
0603:                     if ($sender->{error})
0604:                     {
0605:                         print STDERR
0606:                             "*** Error: $sender->{error}
0607: Email was not sent.
0608: ";
0609:                     }
0610:                     else
0611:                     {
0612:                         print $global_fd_output
0613:                             "Email was sent.
0614: ";
0615:                     }
0616:                 }
0617:                 else
0618:                 {
0619:                     print STDERR
0620:                         "*** Error: Cannot load the perl module Mail::Sender, contact your sysadmin to install this perl module.
0621: (use the shell command \"sudo perl -MCPAN -e 'install Mail::Sender'\")
0622: Email was not sent.
0623: ";
0624:                 }
0625:             }
0626:             else
0627:             {
0628:                 print $global_fd_output
0629:                     "Email was not sent.
0630: ";
0631:             }
0632:         }
0633:         else
0634:         {
0635:             print STDERR "*** Warning: No default route to the internet found.
0636: Email was not sent.
0637: ";
0638:         }
0639:     }
0640:     else
0641:     {
0642:         print $global_fd_output "No email sent.
0643: ";
0644:     }
0645: 
0646:     # if there were errors
0647: 
0648:     if ($global_error_count)
0649:     {
0650:         # exit with failure
0651: 
0652:         $exit_code ||= 1;
0653: 
0654:         print $global_fd_output "$0: $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{command_test} test(s), $global_error_count error(s)\n";
0655: 
0656:         print $global_fd_output "$0: exit_code $exit_code\n\n";
0657: 
0658:         exit $exit_code;
0659:     }
0660: 
0661:     # else
0662: 
0663:     else
0664:     {
0665:         # exit, possibly success
0666: 
0667:         print $global_fd_output "$0: $global_error_count error(s)\n\n";
0668: 
0669:         exit $exit_code;
0670:     }
0671: }
0672: 
0673: 
0674: package ModelLibrary;
0675: 
0676: 
0677: sub sha
0678: {
0679:     # find all models
0680: 
0681:     use File::Find::Rule;
0682: 
0683:     my $files = [ File::Find::Rule->file()->in( $ENV{NEUROSPACES_NMC_MODELS} ), ];
0684: 
0685:     my $shas
0686:         = [
0687:            map
0688:            {
0689:                `sha1sum $_`;
0690:            }
0691:            @$files,
0692:           ];
0693: 
0694:     use Digest::SHA qw(sha1_hex);
0695: 
0696:     my $sha = sha1_hex(join ', ', @$shas);
0697: 
0698:     return $sha;
0699: }
0700: 
0701: 
0702: package Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition;
0703: 
0704: 
0705: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Element");
0706: 
0707: 
0708: sub process_clauses
0709: {
0710:     my $command_definition = shift;
0711: 
0712:     my $execution_context = shift;
0713: 
0714:     my $fd_output = shift;
0715: 
0716:     if (exists $command_definition->{wait})
0717:     {
0718:         my $wait = $command_definition->{wait};
0719: 
0720:         $execution_context->wait($fd_output, $wait);
0721:     }
0722: }
0723: 
0724: 
0725: sub parse_command_line
0726: {
0727:     my $command_definition = shift;
0728: 
0729:     my $command;
0730: 
0731:     my $arguments;
0732: 
0733:     if (exists $command_definition->{command})
0734:     {
0735:         $command = $command_definition->{command};
0736:     }
0737: 
0738:     if (exists $command_definition->{arguments})
0739:     {
0740:         $arguments = $command_definition->{arguments};
0741:     }
0742:     else
0743:     {
0744:         $arguments = [ split '\s', $command, ];
0745: 
0746:         $command = shift @$arguments;
0747:     }
0748: 
0749:     return ($command, $arguments);
0750: }
0751: 
0752: 
0753: sub rebless
0754: {
0755:     my $command_definition = shift;
0756: 
0757:     my $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition";
0758: 
0759:     # we have to either instantiate an object that produces output ...
0760: 
0761:     if ($command_definition->{class})
0762:     {
0763:         $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::PerlClass";
0764:     }
0765: 
0766:     # ... or run perl code that produces output
0767: 
0768:     elsif (ref $command_definition->{command} eq 'CODE')
0769:     {
0770:         $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::PerlCode";
0771:     }
0772: 
0773:     # ... or run a command that produces output
0774: 
0775:     else
0776:     {
0777:         $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::Interactive";
0778:     }
0779: 
0780:     # rebless
0781: 
0782:     bless $command_definition, $target_package;
0783: 
0784:     # return result
0785: 
0786:     return $command_definition;
0787: }
0788: 
0789: 
0790: package Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::Interactive;;
0791: 
0792: 
0793: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition");
0794: 
0795: 
0796: use Data::Comparator qw(data_comparator);
0797: 
0798: 
0799: my $global_exp;
0800: 
0801: my $global_running_command_definition;
0802: 
0803: 
0804: sub close_pending
0805: {
0806:     # if there is a command running
0807: 
0808:     if ($global_exp)
0809:     {
0810:         # terminate the command
0811: 
0812:         #t could be that the hard_close() call is needed because
0813:         #t neurospaces uses readline, not sure needs investigation,
0814:         #t perhaps.
0815: 
0816:         $global_exp->hard_close();
0817:     }
0818: }
0819: 
0820: 
0821: sub spawn_new
0822: {
0823:     my $command_definition = shift;
0824: 
0825:     my $fd_output = shift;
0826: 
0827:     # retrieve the shell command and its arguments
0828: 
0829:     my ($command, $arguments) = $command_definition->parse_command_line();
0830: 
0831:     # compare the running command with this command
0832: 
0833:     my $new_command_definition
0834:         = {
0835:             arguments => $arguments,
0836:             command => $command,
0837:           };
0838: 
0839:     my $differences = data_comparator($new_command_definition, $global_running_command_definition);
0840: 
0841:     # remember to spawn a new command
0842: 
0843:     my $spawn_new = not $command_definition->{recycle};
0844: 
0845:     # if the command can be executed
0846: 
0847:     my $test_startup;
0848: 
0849:     if (defined $command)
0850:     {
0851:         if ($spawn_new)
0852:         {
0853:             if ($global_exp)
0854:             {
0855:                 # terminate the previous command
0856: 
0857:                 #t could be that the hard_close() call is needed because
0858:                 #t neurospaces uses readline, not sure needs investigation,
0859:                 #t perhaps.
0860: 
0861:                 $global_exp->hard_close();
0862:             }
0863: 
0864:             # create a new Expect object by spawning a new process with the new command
0865: 
0866:             $global_exp = Expect->new();
0867: 
0868:             #! see the expect manual for this one
0869: 
0870:             $global_exp->raw_pty(1);
0871: 
0872:             #               $exp->slave->stty(qw(raw -echo));
0873: 
0874:             $global_exp->spawn
0875:                 (
0876:                  (
0877:                   $option_trace
0878:                   ? ("strace", "-f")
0879:                   : ()
0880:                  ),
0881:                  $command,
0882:                  @$arguments
0883:                 )
0884:                 or die "$0: cannot spawn $command: $!\n";
0885: 
0886:             # set the running_command_definition
0887: 
0888:             $global_running_command_definition = $new_command_definition;
0889: 
0890:             print $fd_output "*** Executing $command " . (join ' ', map { "'$_'" } @$arguments) . "\n";
0891: 
0892:             # remember to do startup testing
0893: 
0894:             $test_startup = 1;
0895: 
0896:             # there were no side effects yet
0897: 
0898:             $global_previous_command_side_effects = 0;
0899:         }
0900:         else
0901:         {
0902:             print $fd_output "*** Recycling $command " . (join ' ', map { "'$_'" } @$arguments) . "\n";
0903: 
0904:             $test_startup = 0;
0905:         }
0906:     }
0907: 
0908:     # return the expect object and whether a new command has been started
0909: 
0910:     return $global_exp, $test_startup;
0911: }
0912: 
0913: 
0914: #
0915: # sub start
0916: #
0917: # Starts the application command and connects to its I/O channels.
0918: #
0919: 
0920: sub cd_start
0921: {
0922:     my $self = shift;
0923: 
0924:     my $executor = shift;
0925: 
0926:     my $fd_output = shift;
0927: 
0928:     # start the command and connect with its I/O channels
0929: 
0930:     #! this is similar to having a class clause with value 'Expect' and calling Expect->new()
0931: 
0932:     my ($exp, $test_startup) = $self->spawn_new($fd_output);
0933: 
0934:     $executor->{expect_object} = $exp;
0935: 
0936:     #! I believe this is obsolete
0937: 
0938:     $executor->{test_startup} = $test_startup;
0939: }
0940: 
0941: 
0942: package Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::PerlClass;;
0943: 
0944: 
0945: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition");
0946: 
0947: 
0948: #
0949: # sub start
0950: #
0951: # Calls the new method of the given class to instantiate a Perl object.
0952: #
0953: 
0954: sub cd_start
0955: {
0956:     my $self = shift;
0957: 
0958:     my $executor = shift;
0959: 
0960:     my $fd_output = shift;
0961: 
0962:     # instantiate the object
0963: 
0964:     my $class = $self->{class};
0965: 
0966:     my $filename = $class . ".pm";
0967: 
0968:     $filename =~ s(::)(/)g;
0969: 
0970:     require $filename;
0971: 
0972:     $self->{command_object} = eval "$class->new()";
0973: }
0974: 
0975: 
0976: package Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::PerlCode;
0977: 
0978: 
0979: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition");
0980: 
0981: 
0982: #
0983: # sub start
0984: #
0985: # No-op.
0986: #
0987: # Deprecated: Hardcoded perl code in the test specifications should be removed.
0988: #
0989: 
0990: sub cd_start
0991: {
0992:     my $self = shift;
0993: 
0994:     my $executor = shift;
0995: 
0996:     my $fd_output = shift;
0997: 
0998:     # code is already instantiated, nothing to do here
0999: 
1000:     my $sub = $self->{command};
1001: 
1002:     my $error = &$sub();
1003: 
1004:     if ($error)
1005:     {
1006:         # my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1007: 
1008:         # my $current = $self->_get_current();
1009: 
1010:         # my $module_definition = $current->[0];
1011: 
1012:         # my $command_definition = $current->[1];
1013: 
1014:         # my $command_test = $current->[2];
1015: 
1016:         my $execution_context = $executor->{execution_context};
1017: 
1018:         my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1019: 
1020:         my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
1021: 
1022:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
1023:                 (
1024:                  $fd_output,
1025:                  {
1026:                   description => "command_definition $self->{description}",
1027:                   error => $error,
1028:                   module_name => $module_name,
1029:                  },
1030:                 );
1031:     }
1032: 
1033:     return $error;
1034: }
1035: 
1036: 
1037: package Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::IsFileExecutable;
1038: 
1039: 
1040: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition");
1041: 
1042: 
1043: #
1044: # sub start
1045: #
1046: 
1047: sub cd_start
1048: {
1049:     my $self = shift;
1050: 
1051:     my $executor = shift;
1052: 
1053:     my $fd_output = shift;
1054: 
1055:     # code is already instantiated, nothing to do here
1056: 
1057:     my $sub = $self->{command};
1058: 
1059:     my $error;
1060: 
1061:     if (-x "workflow-tests-commands-data/examples_sh/sh_single_command.sh")
1062:     {
1063:     }
1064:     else
1065:     {
1066:         $error = "workflow-tests-commands-data/examples_sh/sh_single_command.sh does not have its execute bit set"
1067:     }
1068: 
1069:     if ($error)
1070:     {
1071:         # my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1072: 
1073:         # my $current = $self->_get_current();
1074: 
1075:         # my $module_definition = $current->[0];
1076: 
1077:         # my $command_definition = $current->[1];
1078: 
1079:         # my $command_test = $current->[2];
1080: 
1081:         my $execution_context = $executor->{execution_context};
1082: 
1083:         my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1084: 
1085:         my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
1086: 
1087:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
1088:                 (
1089:                  $fd_output,
1090:                  {
1091:                   description => "Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition::IsFileExecutable $self->{description}",
1092:                   error => $error,
1093:                   module_name => $module_name,
1094:                  },
1095:                 );
1096:     }
1097: 
1098:     return $error;
1099: }
1100: 
1101: 
1102: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest;
1103: 
1104: 
1105: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Element");
1106: 
1107: 
1108: #
1109: # _after_run()
1110: #
1111: # 1. records the time it took to execute the test.
1112: # 2. calls approximate testers if this is allowed by options.
1113: #
1114: 
1115: sub _after_run
1116: {
1117:     my $self = shift;
1118: 
1119:     my $execution_context = shift;
1120: 
1121:     my $test_result = shift;
1122: 
1123:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1124: 
1125:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1126: 
1127:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
1128: 
1129:     my $time_start = $self->{time_start};
1130: 
1131:     my $time_elapsed = tv_interval($time_start);
1132: 
1133:     # fill in the elapsed time in the report
1134: 
1135:     if ($option_timings)
1136:     {
1137:         my $module_definition = $self->{module_definition};
1138: 
1139:         my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
1140: 
1141:         my $description = $self->{description};
1142: 
1143:         $global_test_report->{timings}->{$module_name}->{$description} = $time_elapsed;
1144:     }
1145: 
1146:     # attempt to correct if the result is almost correct
1147: 
1148:     #! perl code does not produce a full test result object, so we cannot try to correct an almost correct result
1149: 
1150:     if (not (ref $command eq 'CODE'))
1151:     {
1152:         # if things don't match
1153: 
1154:         if ($test_result->{error})
1155:         {
1156:             my $module_definition = $self->{module_definition};
1157: 
1158:             # if allowed to compare numerically
1159: 
1160:             if ((!$self->{string_only}
1161:                  && $option_numerical_compare)
1162: 
1163:                 # or forced to compare numerically
1164: 
1165:                 || $self->{numerical_compare}
1166:                 || $command_definition->{numerical_compare}
1167:                 || $module_definition->{numerical_compare})
1168:             {
1169:                 # compare numerically
1170: 
1171:                 push @{$global_test_report->{numerical_compare}}, $self->{description};
1172: 
1173:                 #! this call uses the $read variable, making it harder to bring this call
1174:                 #! outside the condition that tests if this variable is defined
1175:                 #! this variable contains the expected output
1176: 
1177:                 $test_result->{error}
1178:                     = Neurospaces::Tester::Comparators::numerical
1179:                         (
1180:                          {
1181:                           description => $self->{description},
1182:                          },
1183:                          $test_result->{before_match},
1184:                          $test_result->{expected},
1185:                          $test_result->{error});
1186:             }
1187:         }
1188:     }
1189: 
1190:     # return result
1191: 
1192:     return $test_result;
1193: }
1194: 
1195: 
1196: sub after_testing
1197: {
1198:     my $self = shift;
1199: 
1200:     my $execution_context = shift;
1201: 
1202:     my $test_result = shift;
1203: 
1204:     return $self->_after_run($execution_context, $test_result);
1205: }
1206: 
1207: 
1208: sub _before_run
1209: {
1210:     my $self = shift;
1211: 
1212:     my $execution_context = shift;
1213: 
1214:     my $fd_output = shift;
1215: 
1216:     # record time
1217: 
1218:     use Time::HiRes qw(gettimeofday tv_interval);
1219: 
1220:     my $time_start = [ gettimeofday(), ];
1221: 
1222:     $self->{time_start} = $time_start;
1223: }
1224: 
1225: 
1226: #
1227: # sub before_testing()
1228: #
1229: # Calls ->_before_run() to record the current time.
1230: #
1231: # This method is overriden in the Interactive package.
1232: #
1233: 
1234: sub before_testing
1235: {
1236:     my $self = shift;
1237: 
1238:     my $execution_context = shift;
1239: 
1240:     my $fd_output = shift;
1241: 
1242:     # record time
1243: 
1244:     $self->_before_run($execution_context, $fd_output);
1245: }
1246: 
1247: 
1248: sub rebless
1249: {
1250:     my $command_test = shift;
1251: 
1252:     my $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest";
1253: 
1254:     if (ref $command_test->{write} eq 'ARRAY')
1255:     {
1256:         # command_object
1257: 
1258:         $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::CommandObject";
1259: 
1260:         my $writes = $command_test->{write};
1261: 
1262:         foreach my $write (@$writes)
1263:         {
1264:             my $method = $write->{method};
1265: 
1266:             my $arguments = $write->{arguments};
1267:         }
1268: 
1269:         # compare the read data with what is expected
1270: 
1271:         if (exists $command_test->{read})
1272:         {
1273:             my $read = $command_test->{read};
1274:         }
1275:     }
1276: 
1277:     # if the command_definition has executable perl code
1278: 
1279:     # elsif (ref $command eq 'CODE')
1280:     # {
1281:         # $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::PerlCode";
1282: 
1283:     # }
1284: 
1285:     # else the command definition runs a shell command
1286: 
1287:     else
1288:     {
1289:         # set read, wait and write strings
1290: 
1291:         $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive";
1292: 
1293:         my $read = $command_test->{read};
1294: 
1295:         my $wait = $command_test->{wait};
1296: 
1297:         my $write = $command_test->{write};
1298: 
1299:         # set timeout, defaults to two seconds, but for newly created processes add two additional seconds.
1300: 
1301:         my $timeout
1302:             = (defined $command_test->{timeout}
1303:                ? $command_test->{timeout} * $option_timeout_multiplier
1304:                : (0
1305:                   ? 4 * $option_timeout_multiplier
1306:                   : 2 * $option_timeout_multiplier));
1307: 
1308:         # write
1309: 
1310:         if (ref $write eq 'HASH')
1311:         {
1312:         }
1313:         elsif (defined $write)
1314:         {
1315:             # send write to the command under test via the expect object
1316:         }
1317: 
1318:         # wait
1319: 
1320:         if ($wait)
1321:         {
1322:             # wait for $wait seconds
1323:         }
1324: 
1325:         # check the shell command that will be executed with a timeout
1326: 
1327:         my $shell = $command_test->{shell};
1328: 
1329:         if (ref $shell eq 'HASH')
1330:         {
1331:             #! not sure what this can be
1332:         }
1333:         elsif (defined $shell)
1334:         {
1335:             # run the command for a maximum of $timeout seconds
1336: 
1337:             # fall through
1338:         }
1339: 
1340:         # if there is an external application to test the final output
1341: 
1342:         #! the previous shell command was a post-processor, this one is a tester
1343: 
1344:         my $tester = $command_test->{tester};
1345: 
1346:         if ($tester)
1347:         {
1348:             $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::ShellTester";
1349: 
1350:             # set the external tester that compares the generated output with what is expected
1351: 
1352:             #! the external tester runs an executable shell command
1353: 
1354:             my $shell = $tester->{shell};
1355: 
1356:             # there must be a shell command we should run
1357: 
1358:             if ($shell)
1359:             {
1360:                 my $produced = `$shell`;
1361: 
1362:                 my $test_result
1363:                     = {
1364:                        expected => defined $tester->{expected} ? $tester->{expected} : '',
1365:                       };
1366: 
1367:                 if (($produced eq ''
1368:                      and $test_result->{expected} ne '')
1369:                     or ($produced ne ''
1370:                         and $test_result->{expected} eq ''))
1371:                 {
1372:                     $test_result->{error} = "external tester produces output that is different from what is expected, one is empty, the other is not";
1373: 
1374:                     $test_result->{before_match} = $produced;
1375:                 }
1376:                 elsif ($produced =~ /$test_result->{expected}/)
1377:                 {
1378:                 }
1379:                 else
1380:                 {
1381:                     $test_result->{error} = "the external tester produces output that is different from the expected output";
1382: 
1383:                     $test_result->{before_match} = $produced;
1384:                 }
1385:             }
1386:             else
1387:             {
1388:                 die "$0: *** Error: external testers must have a shell command";
1389:             }
1390:         }
1391: 
1392:         # read
1393: 
1394:         elsif (defined $read)
1395:         {
1396:             # if literal text expected
1397: 
1398:             if (!ref $read)
1399:             {
1400:                 if (exists $command_test->{white_space})
1401:                 {
1402:                     if ($command_test->{white_space} eq 'convert seen 0a to 0d 0a newlines')
1403:                     {
1404:                         $read =~ s(\x0a)(\x0d\x0a)g;
1405:                     }
1406:                 }
1407: 
1408:                 $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Literal";
1409: 
1410:                 # expect $read for output
1411:             }
1412: 
1413:             # if array, means regex match
1414: 
1415:             elsif (ref $read eq 'ARRAY')
1416:             {
1417:                 # expect $read to match as a regex with the output
1418: 
1419:                 $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Regex";
1420: 
1421:             }
1422: 
1423:             # else, hash: one of several alternatives expected
1424: 
1425:             else
1426:             {
1427:                 # compose the alternatives regex
1428: 
1429:                 if ($read->{alternatives})
1430:                 {
1431:                     # expect that the output literally matches with one of the given alternatives
1432: 
1433:                     $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Alternatives";
1434: 
1435:                     my $alternatives = $read->{alternatives};
1436:                 }
1437: 
1438:                 # if an application output file is expected
1439: 
1440:                 elsif ($read->{application_output_file})
1441:                 {
1442:                     $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::File";
1443: 
1444:                     # read it
1445: 
1446:                     my $application_output_file = $read->{application_output_file};
1447: 
1448:                     # and literally compare it with an expected output file or with the {expected_output}
1449: 
1450:                     my $expected_output_file = $read->{expected_output_file};
1451: 
1452:                     local $/;
1453: 
1454:                     my $application_output = `cat "$application_output_file"`;
1455: 
1456:                     if ($expected_output_file)
1457:                     {
1458:                     }
1459:                     elsif (defined $read->{expected_output})
1460:                     {
1461:                     }
1462:                 }
1463: 
1464:                 # if there is a shell command given
1465: 
1466:                 elsif ($read->{shell})
1467:                 {
1468:                     $target_package = "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Shell";
1469: 
1470:                     # run it and capture its output
1471: 
1472:                     my $shell = $read->{shell};
1473: 
1474:                     # and compare its output with the output of the application
1475:                 }
1476:                 else
1477:                 {
1478:                     my $description = $command_test->{description};
1479: 
1480:                     die "test not understood by $0, aborting (illegal read clause for $description)";
1481:                 }
1482:             }
1483:         }
1484:     }
1485: 
1486:     # rebless
1487: 
1488:     bless $command_test, $target_package;
1489: 
1490:     # return result
1491: 
1492:     return $command_test;
1493: }
1494: 
1495: 
1496: #
1497: # run()
1498: #
1499: # The default implementation executes perl code in the command definition.
1500: #
1501: 
1502: sub ct_run
1503: {
1504:     my $self = shift;
1505: 
1506:     my $execution_context = shift;
1507: 
1508:     my $executor = shift;
1509: 
1510:     my $fd_output = shift;
1511: 
1512:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1513: 
1514:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1515: 
1516:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
1517: 
1518:     # expect this output
1519: 
1520:     my $test_result;
1521: 
1522:     # if the command_definition has executable perl code
1523: 
1524:     if (ref $command eq 'CODE')
1525:     {
1526:         # execute the perl code
1527: 
1528:         use Cwd;
1529: 
1530:         my $directory = getcwd();
1531: 
1532:         my $error
1533:             = &$command
1534:             (
1535:              $self,
1536:              {
1537:               c_code => {
1538:                          directory => $directory,
1539:                         },
1540:              },
1541:             );
1542: 
1543:         $test_result
1544:             = {
1545:                error => $error,
1546:               };
1547:     }
1548: 
1549:     # return result
1550: 
1551:     return $test_result;
1552: }
1553: 
1554: 
1555: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::CommandObject;
1556: 
1557: 
1558: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest");
1559: 
1560: 
1561: #
1562: # run()
1563: #
1564: # write the write clauses of the command test to the test object.
1565: # expect the contents of the read clause for output.
1566: #
1567: 
1568: sub ct_run
1569: {
1570:     my $self = shift;
1571: 
1572:     my $execution_context = shift;
1573: 
1574:     my $executor = shift;
1575: 
1576:     my $fd_output = shift;
1577: 
1578:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1579: 
1580:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1581: 
1582:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
1583: 
1584:     # expect this output
1585: 
1586:     my $test_result;
1587: 
1588:     # record time
1589: 
1590:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $fd_output);
1591: 
1592:     # if the command_definition instantiated an object
1593: 
1594:     my $command_object = $executor->{command_object};
1595: 
1596:     # write data to the object using the given methods
1597:     # and arguments and obtain a result from the
1598:     # object
1599: 
1600:     my $result;
1601: 
1602:     my $last_method;
1603: 
1604:     my $writes = $self->{write};
1605: 
1606:     foreach my $write (@$writes)
1607:     {
1608:         my $method = $write->{method};
1609: 
1610:         $last_method = $method;
1611: 
1612:         my $arguments = $write->{arguments};
1613: 
1614:         $result = $command_object->$method(@$arguments);
1615:     }
1616: 
1617:     # compare the read data with what is expected
1618: 
1619:     if (exists $self->{read})
1620:     {
1621:         my $read = $self->{read};
1622: 
1623:         $test_result
1624:             = {
1625:                error => ($result eq $read
1626:                          ? ""
1627:                          : "$last_method returned $result, expected $read"),
1628:                before_match => $result,
1629:                expected => $read,
1630:               };
1631:     }
1632: 
1633:     # try correcting
1634: 
1635:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
1636: 
1637:     # return the final test result
1638: 
1639:     return $test_result;
1640: }
1641: 
1642: 
1643: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive;
1644: 
1645: #
1646: # The interactive package provides methods for testing interactive
1647: # commands:
1648: #
1649: # - waiting for predetermined amount of time.
1650: #
1651: # - post processing of application results before the test takes
1652: #   place.
1653: #
1654: # - record execution times.
1655: #
1656: # It overrides the ->before_testing() method to conveniently call some
1657: # of these methods.
1658: #
1659: # Derived packages should only override the ->run() method.
1660: #
1661: 
1662: 
1663: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest");
1664: 
1665: 
1666: sub before_testing
1667: {
1668:     my $self = shift;
1669: 
1670:     my $execution_context = shift;
1671: 
1672:     my $executor = shift;
1673: 
1674:     my $fd_output = shift;
1675: 
1676:     # record time
1677: 
1678:     $self->_before_run($execution_context, $fd_output);
1679: 
1680:     # do write processing
1681: 
1682:     $self->_process_write($executor, $fd_output);
1683: 
1684:     # do wait processing
1685: 
1686:     $self->_process_wait($execution_context, $fd_output);
1687: 
1688:     # do post processing
1689: 
1690:     $self->_prepare_run($execution_context, $fd_output);
1691: }
1692: 
1693: 
1694: # compute a sensible timeout for interaction with the command that is
1695: # being tested.
1696: 
1697: sub get_timeout
1698: {
1699:     my $self = shift;
1700: 
1701:     my $test_startup = $self->{test_startup};
1702: 
1703:     my $result
1704:         = (defined $self->{timeout}
1705:            ? $self->{timeout} * $option_timeout_multiplier
1706:            : ($test_startup
1707:               ? 4 * $option_timeout_multiplier
1708:               : 2 * $option_timeout_multiplier));
1709: 
1710:     return $result;
1711: }
1712: 
1713: 
1714: #
1715: # _prepare_run()
1716: #
1717: # Provides processing of a 'shell' clause to post-process results
1718: # generated by the application.
1719: #
1720: 
1721: sub _prepare_run
1722: {
1723:     my $self = shift;
1724: 
1725:     my $execution_context = shift;
1726: 
1727:     my $fd_output = shift;
1728: 
1729:     # check the shell command that will be executed with a timeout
1730: 
1731:     my $shell = $self->{shell};
1732: 
1733:     if (ref $shell eq 'HASH')
1734:     {
1735:         #! not sure what this can be
1736:     }
1737:     elsif (defined $shell)
1738:     {
1739:         # run the command for a maximum of $timeout seconds
1740: 
1741:         # allow it to post-process results generated by the command that is being tested
1742: 
1743:         my $timeout = $self->get_timeout();
1744: 
1745:         print $fd_output "*** Shell: timeout $timeout $shell\n";
1746: 
1747:         system "timeout $timeout $shell";
1748: 
1749:         #t check the output or status of the shell command?
1750: 
1751:         # fall through
1752:     }
1753: 
1754:     #t return something sensible
1755: 
1756:     return $?;
1757: }
1758: 
1759: 
1760: sub _process_wait
1761: {
1762:     my $self = shift;
1763: 
1764:     my $execution_context = shift;
1765: 
1766:     my $fd_output = shift;
1767: 
1768:     if (exists $self->{wait})
1769:     {
1770:         my $wait = $self->{wait};
1771: 
1772:         $execution_context->wait($fd_output, $wait);
1773:     }
1774: }
1775: 
1776: 
1777: sub _process_write
1778: {
1779:     my $self = shift;
1780: 
1781:     my $executor = shift;
1782: 
1783:     my $fd_output = shift;
1784: 
1785:     my $write = $self->{write};
1786: 
1787:     # write
1788: 
1789:     if (ref $write eq 'HASH')
1790:     {
1791:     }
1792:     elsif (defined $write)
1793:     {
1794:         print $fd_output "*** Write: $write\n";
1795: 
1796:         $executor->{expect_object}->send("$write\n");
1797:     }
1798: }
1799: 
1800: 
1801: # I believe this is only a template for derived classes.
1802: 
1803: sub ct_run
1804: {
1805:     my $self = shift;
1806: 
1807:     my $execution_context = shift;
1808: 
1809:     my $executor = shift;
1810: 
1811:     my $fd_output = shift;
1812: 
1813:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1814: 
1815:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1816: 
1817:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
1818: 
1819:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
1820: 
1821:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
1822: 
1823:     # try correcting
1824: 
1825:     my $test_result;
1826: 
1827:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
1828: 
1829:     # return the final test result
1830: 
1831:     return $test_result;
1832: }
1833: 
1834: 
1835: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::ShellTester;
1836: 
1837: 
1838: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive");
1839: 
1840: 
1841: # write the write clauses of the command test.
1842: # use the shell command in the tester clause to produce the application output.
1843: #   the shell command should find the produced output and process it.
1844: # compare this output with the expected output.
1845: 
1846: sub ct_run
1847: {
1848:     my $self = shift;
1849: 
1850:     my $execution_context = shift;
1851: 
1852:     my $executor = shift;
1853: 
1854:     my $fd_output = shift;
1855: 
1856:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1857: 
1858:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1859: 
1860:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
1861: 
1862:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
1863: 
1864:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
1865: 
1866:     # if there is an external application to test the final output
1867: 
1868:     #! the previous shell command was a post-processor, this one is a tester
1869: 
1870:     my $tester = $self->{tester};
1871: 
1872:     # set the external tester that compares the generated output with what is expected
1873: 
1874:     #! the external tester runs an executable shell command
1875: 
1876:     my $shell = $tester->{shell};
1877: 
1878:     my $produced = `$shell`;
1879: 
1880:     my $test_result
1881:         = {
1882:            expected => defined $tester->{expected} ? $tester->{expected} : '',
1883:           };
1884: 
1885:     #                       if ($expected)
1886:     #                           $expected = quotemeta $tester->{expected};
1887: 
1888:     if (($produced eq ''
1889:          and $test_result->{expected} ne '')
1890:         or ($produced ne ''
1891:             and $test_result->{expected} eq ''))
1892:     {
1893:         $test_result->{error}
1894:             = "external tester produces output that is different from what is expected, one is empty, the other is not";
1895: 
1896:         $test_result->{before_match} = $produced;
1897:     }
1898:     elsif ($produced =~ /$test_result->{expected}/)
1899:     {
1900:     }
1901:     else
1902:     {
1903:         $test_result->{error} = "the external tester produces output that is different from the expected output";
1904: 
1905:         $test_result->{before_match} = $produced;
1906:     }
1907: 
1908:     # try correcting
1909: 
1910:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
1911: 
1912:     # return the final test result
1913: 
1914:     return $test_result;
1915: }
1916: 
1917: 
1918: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Literal;
1919: 
1920: 
1921: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive");
1922: 
1923: 
1924: # write the write clauses of the command test.
1925: # read the application output using the expect object.
1926: # literally compare the application output with the expected output.
1927: 
1928: sub ct_run
1929: {
1930:     my $self = shift;
1931: 
1932:     my $execution_context = shift;
1933: 
1934:     my $executor = shift;
1935: 
1936:     my $fd_output = shift;
1937: 
1938:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
1939: 
1940:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
1941: 
1942:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
1943: 
1944:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
1945: 
1946:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
1947: 
1948:     # set the expected output
1949: 
1950:     my $read = $self->{read};
1951: 
1952:     # if literal text expected
1953: 
1954:     if (exists $self->{white_space})
1955:     {
1956:         if ($self->{white_space} eq 'convert seen 0a to 0d 0a newlines')
1957:         {
1958:             print $fd_output "*** Converting seen 0a to 0d 0a newlines\n";
1959: 
1960:             $read =~ s(\x0a)(\x0d\x0a)g;
1961:         }
1962:     }
1963: 
1964:     my $timeout = $self->get_timeout();
1965: 
1966:     my ($matched_pattern_position,
1967:         $error,
1968:         $successfully_matching_string,
1969:         $before_match,
1970:         $after_match)
1971:         = $executor->{expect_object}->expect($timeout, $read, );
1972: 
1973:     my $test_result
1974:         = {
1975:            matched_pattern_position => $matched_pattern_position,
1976:            error => $error,
1977:            successfully_matching_string => $successfully_matching_string,
1978:            before_match => $before_match,
1979:            after_match => $after_match,
1980:            expected => $read,
1981:           };
1982: 
1983:     # try correcting
1984: 
1985:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
1986: 
1987:     # return the final test result
1988: 
1989:     return $test_result;
1990: }
1991: 
1992: 
1993: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Regex;
1994: 
1995: 
1996: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive");
1997: 
1998: 
1999: # write the write clauses of the command test.
2000: # read the application output using the expect object.
2001: # compare the application output with the expected output as a regex.
2002: 
2003: sub ct_run
2004: {
2005:     my $self = shift;
2006: 
2007:     my $execution_context = shift;
2008: 
2009:     my $executor = shift;
2010: 
2011:     my $fd_output = shift;
2012: 
2013:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
2014: 
2015:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
2016: 
2017:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
2018: 
2019:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
2020: 
2021:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
2022: 
2023:     # set the expected output
2024: 
2025:     my $read = $self->{read};
2026: 
2027:     # if array, means regex match
2028: 
2029:     my $timeout = $self->get_timeout();
2030: 
2031:     my ($matched_pattern_position,
2032:         $error,
2033:         $successfully_matching_string,
2034:         $before_match,
2035:         $after_match)
2036:         = $executor->{expect_object}->expect($timeout, @$read, );
2037: 
2038:     my $test_result
2039:         = {
2040:            matched_pattern_position => $matched_pattern_position,
2041:            error => $error,
2042:            successfully_matching_string => $successfully_matching_string,
2043:            before_match => $before_match,
2044:            after_match => $after_match,
2045: 
2046:            #! skip the expect '-re' flag
2047: 
2048:            expected => $read->[1],
2049:           };
2050: 
2051:     # try correcting
2052: 
2053:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
2054: 
2055:     # return the final test result
2056: 
2057:     return $test_result;
2058: }
2059: 
2060: 
2061: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Alternatives;
2062: 
2063: 
2064: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive");
2065: 
2066: 
2067: # write the write clauses of the command test.
2068: # read the application output using the expect object.
2069: # compare the application output with the expected output which is a list of alternatives.
2070: 
2071: sub ct_run
2072: {
2073:     my $self = shift;
2074: 
2075:     my $execution_context = shift;
2076: 
2077:     my $executor = shift;
2078: 
2079:     my $fd_output = shift;
2080: 
2081:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
2082: 
2083:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
2084: 
2085:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
2086: 
2087:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
2088: 
2089:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
2090: 
2091:     # compose the alternatives regex
2092: 
2093:     my $read = $self->{read};
2094: 
2095:     my $alternatives = $read->{alternatives};
2096: 
2097:     my $test_result
2098:         = {
2099:            expected => '(' . (join '|', map { quotemeta } @$alternatives) . ')',
2100:           };
2101: 
2102:     # read from the application
2103: 
2104:     my $timeout = $self->get_timeout();
2105: 
2106:     my ($matched_pattern_position,
2107:         $error,
2108:         $successfully_matching_string,
2109:         $before_match,
2110:         $after_match)
2111:         = $executor->{expect_object}->expect($timeout, "-re", $test_result->{expected}, );
2112: 
2113:     $test_result->{matched_pattern_position} = $matched_pattern_position;
2114:     $test_result->{error} = $error;
2115:     $test_result->{successfully_matching_string} = $successfully_matching_string;
2116:     $test_result->{before_match} = $before_match;
2117:     $test_result->{after_match} = $after_match;
2118: 
2119:     # try correcting
2120: 
2121:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
2122: 
2123:     # return the final test result
2124: 
2125:     return $test_result;
2126: }
2127: 
2128: 
2129: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::File;
2130: 
2131: 
2132: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive");
2133: 
2134: 
2135: # write the write clauses of the command test.
2136: # read the application output from a file.
2137: # compare the application output with the expected output which is a file.
2138: 
2139: sub ct_run
2140: {
2141:     my $self = shift;
2142: 
2143:     my $execution_context = shift;
2144: 
2145:     my $executor = shift;
2146: 
2147:     my $fd_output = shift;
2148: 
2149:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
2150: 
2151:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
2152: 
2153:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
2154: 
2155:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
2156: 
2157:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
2158: 
2159:     # read the application output file
2160: 
2161:     my $read = $self->{read};
2162: 
2163:     my $application_output_file = $read->{application_output_file};
2164: 
2165:     my $pwd = `pwd`;
2166: 
2167:     chomp $pwd;
2168: 
2169:     print $fd_output "*** Application output file: $application_output_file (in $pwd)\n";
2170: 
2171:     # and compare it with the expected output file
2172: 
2173:     my $expected_output_file = $read->{expected_output_file};
2174: 
2175:     local $/;
2176: 
2177:     my $application_output = `cat "$application_output_file"`;
2178: 
2179:     my $expected_output;
2180: 
2181:     if ($expected_output_file)
2182:     {
2183:         print $fd_output "*** Expected output file: $expected_output_file\n";
2184: 
2185:         $expected_output = `cat "$expected_output_file"`;
2186:     }
2187:     elsif (defined $read->{expected_output})
2188:     {
2189:         $expected_output = $read->{expected_output};
2190:     }
2191: 
2192:     my $test_result
2193:         = {
2194:            expected => $expected_output,
2195:           };
2196: 
2197:     if ($expected_output eq $application_output)
2198:     {
2199:         $test_result->{before_match} = $application_output;
2200:     }
2201:     else
2202:     {
2203:         $test_result->{before_match} = $application_output;
2204: 
2205:         $read = $expected_output;
2206: 
2207:         $test_result->{error} = 'expected_output does not match application_output';
2208:     }
2209: 
2210:     # try correcting
2211: 
2212:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
2213: 
2214:     # return the final test result
2215: 
2216:     return $test_result;
2217: }
2218: 
2219: 
2220: package Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive::Shell;
2221: 
2222: 
2223: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest::Interactive");
2224: 
2225: 
2226: # write the write clauses of the command test.
2227: # run the given shell command and capture its output.
2228: # read the application output using the expect object.
2229: # compare this output with the application output.
2230: 
2231: sub ct_run
2232: {
2233:     my $self = shift;
2234: 
2235:     my $execution_context = shift;
2236: 
2237:     my $executor = shift;
2238: 
2239:     my $fd_output = shift;
2240: 
2241:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
2242: 
2243:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
2244: 
2245:     my $command = $command_definition->{command};
2246: 
2247:     # record time, do write, wait processing, pre-processing
2248: 
2249:     $self->before_testing($execution_context, $executor, $fd_output);
2250: 
2251:     # run the given shell command and capture its output
2252: 
2253:     my $read = $self->{read};
2254: 
2255:     my $shell = $read->{shell};
2256: 
2257:     my $expected = `$shell`;
2258: 
2259:     # and compare its output with the output of the application
2260: 
2261:     my $timeout = $self->get_timeout();
2262: 
2263:     my ($matched_pattern_position,
2264:         $error,
2265:         $successfully_matching_string,
2266:         $before_match,
2267:         $after_match)
2268:         = $executor->{expect_object}->expect($timeout, $expected, );
2269: 
2270:     my $test_result
2271:         = {
2272:            matched_pattern_position => $matched_pattern_position,
2273:            error => $error,
2274:            successfully_matching_string => $successfully_matching_string,
2275:            before_match => $before_match,
2276:            after_match => $after_match,
2277:            expected => $expected,
2278:           };
2279: 
2280:     # try correcting
2281: 
2282:     $self->after_testing($execution_context, $test_result);
2283: 
2284:     # return the final test result
2285: 
2286:     return $test_result;
2287: }
2288: 
2289: 
2290: package Heterarch::Test::Element;
2291: 
2292: 
2293: our $selected_output_levels;
2294: 
2295: 
2296: # sub is_selected() should implement a logical and of the different selection criteria
2297: 
2298: sub is_selected
2299: {
2300:     my $self = shift;
2301: 
2302:     my $fd_output = shift;
2303: 
2304:     return $self->is_selected_by_output_level_options($fd_output);
2305: }
2306: 
2307: 
2308: sub is_selected_by_output_level_options
2309: {
2310:     my $self = shift;
2311: 
2312:     my $fd_output = shift;
2313: 
2314:     # map the selected output levels to a hash to facilitate easy detection what has been enabled
2315: 
2316:     if (not defined $selected_output_levels)
2317:     {
2318:         $selected_output_levels
2319:             = {
2320:                map
2321:                {
2322:                    my $output_level = $_;
2323: 
2324:                    my $result = { $output_level => "from options", };
2325: 
2326:                    %$result;
2327:                }
2328:                @$option_output_levels,
2329:               };
2330:     }
2331: 
2332:     # convert the class to its output level
2333: 
2334:     use Scalar::Util 'blessed';
2335: 
2336:     my $type = defined blessed $self && $self->isa("Heterarch::Test::Module") ? "module" : "";
2337: 
2338:     $type ||= defined blessed $self && $self->isa("Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition") ? "command_definition" : "";
2339: 
2340:     $type ||= defined blessed $self && $self->isa("Heterarch::Test::CommandTest") ? "command_test" : "";
2341: 
2342:     # determine whether this output level was selected
2343: 
2344:     my $selected = $selected_output_levels->{$type};
2345: 
2346:     # return result
2347: 
2348:     return $selected;
2349: }
2350: 
2351: 
2352: package Heterarch::Test::ExecutionContext;
2353: 
2354: 
2355: #
2356: # _prepre_engine() implements the engine for both preparation and
2357: # reparation that are used for test modules and command definitions.
2358: #
2359: 
2360: sub _prepre_engine
2361: {
2362:     my $executor_element = shift;
2363: 
2364:     my $description = shift;
2365: 
2366:     my $previous_result = shift;
2367: 
2368:     my $result = 'nothing executed';
2369: 
2370:     # either execute regular perl code
2371: 
2372:     if (ref $executor_element eq 'CODE')
2373:     {
2374:         $result = &$executor_element($previous_result);
2375:     }
2376: 
2377:     # ... or instantiate the object that will do the preparation
2378: 
2379:     elsif (exists $executor_element->{class})
2380:     {
2381:         # instantiate the object
2382: 
2383:         my $class = $executor_element->{class};
2384: 
2385:         my $filename = $class . ".pm";
2386: 
2387:         $filename =~ s(::)(/)g;
2388: 
2389:         require $filename;
2390: 
2391:         my $executor_element_object = eval "$class->new()";
2392: 
2393:         # apply all the methods and collect the results
2394: 
2395:         $result = [];
2396: 
2397:         my $applicators = $executor_element->{applicators};
2398: 
2399:         foreach my $applicator (@$applicators)
2400:         {
2401:             my $method = $applicator->{method};
2402: 
2403:             my $arguments = $applicator->{arguments};
2404: 
2405:             my $applicator_result = $executor_element_object->$method($arguments);
2406: 
2407:             if (ref $applicator_result eq 'SCALAR')
2408:             {
2409:                 $result = \ "Error: $description failed ($$applicator_result)";
2410: 
2411:                 last;
2412:             }
2413: 
2414:             push @$result, $applicator_result;
2415:         }
2416:     }
2417: 
2418:     # or execute an array of system shell commands
2419: 
2420:     elsif (exists $executor_element->{system_commands})
2421:     {
2422:         my $system_commands = $executor_element->{system_commands};
2423: 
2424:         if (ref $system_commands eq 'ARRAY')
2425:         {
2426:             foreach my $system_command (@$system_commands)
2427:             {
2428:                 system $system_command;
2429: 
2430:                 if ($? ne 0)
2431:                 {
2432:                     $result = \ "Error: $description failed ($system_command)";
2433: 
2434:                     last;
2435:                 }
2436:             }
2437:         }
2438:     }
2439: 
2440:     # return result
2441: 
2442:     return $result;
2443: }
2444: 
2445: 
2446: sub _get_current
2447: {
2448:     my $self = shift;
2449: 
2450:     my $path = $self->{path};
2451: 
2452:     my $current = $path->[$#$path];
2453: 
2454:     return $current;
2455: }
2456: 
2457: 
2458: #t to be replaced with instance variables
2459: 
2460: our $command_definition_counter_per_module = 0;
2461: our $command_definition_counter_global = 0;
2462: 
2463: our $command_test_counter_per_command_definition = 0;
2464: our $command_test_counter_per_module = 0;
2465: our $command_test_counter_global = 0;
2466: 
2467: our $module_counter_global = 0;
2468: 
2469: 
2470: sub _maintain_counters
2471: {
2472:     my $self = shift;
2473: 
2474:     my $current = shift;
2475: 
2476:     my $command_definition = $current->[1];
2477: 
2478:     my $command_test = $current->[2];
2479: 
2480:     my $module_definition = $current->[0];
2481: 
2482:     if (defined $command_test)
2483:     {
2484:         $command_test_counter_global++;
2485:         $command_test_counter_per_module++;
2486:         $command_test_counter_per_command_definition++;
2487:     }
2488:     elsif (defined $command_definition)
2489:     {
2490:         $command_definition_counter_global++;
2491:         $command_definition_counter_per_module++;
2492: 
2493:         $command_test_counter_per_command_definition = 0;
2494:     }
2495:     elsif (defined $module_definition)
2496:     {
2497:         $module_counter_global++;
2498: 
2499:         $command_definition_counter_per_module = 0;
2500:         $command_test_counter_per_module = 0;
2501:     }
2502:     else
2503:     {
2504:         die "$0: internal error when _maintain_counters(): nothing left to count\n";
2505:     }
2506: }
2507: 
2508: 
2509: sub _push_current
2510: {
2511:     my $self = shift;
2512: 
2513:     my $current = shift;
2514: 
2515:     my $path = $self->{path};
2516: 
2517:     push @$path, $current;
2518: 
2519:     $self->_maintain_counters($current);
2520: 
2521: }
2522: 
2523: 
2524: sub _pop_current
2525: {
2526:     my $self = shift;
2527: 
2528:     my $path = $self->{path};
2529: 
2530:     my $result = pop @$path;
2531: 
2532:     return $result;
2533: }
2534: 
2535: 
2536: sub _push_preparation_result
2537: {
2538:     my $self = shift;
2539: 
2540:     my $preparation_result = shift;
2541: 
2542:     my $path = $self->{preparation_result};
2543: 
2544:     push @$path, $preparation_result;
2545: 
2546: }
2547: 
2548: 
2549: sub _pop_preparation_result
2550: {
2551:     my $self = shift;
2552: 
2553:     my $path = $self->{preparation_result};
2554: 
2555:     my $result = pop @$path;
2556: 
2557:     return $result;
2558: }
2559: 
2560: 
2561: sub ec_end
2562: {
2563:     my $self = shift;
2564: 
2565:     my $module_definition = shift;
2566: 
2567:     my $command_definition = shift;
2568: 
2569:     my $command_test = shift;
2570: 
2571:     #! note: reparation_error has already been processed
2572: 
2573:     my $reparation_error = $self->_reparation();
2574: 
2575:     if ($global_test_report->{output_format} eq 'executor')
2576:     {
2577:         my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
2578: 
2579:         my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
2580: 
2581:         my $description;
2582: 
2583:         my $name;
2584: 
2585:         if (defined $command_test)
2586:         {
2587:             $description = $command_test->{description} ? "*** Test: $command_test->{description}" : "(Warning: no description of this command_test when ending the execution context)";
2588: 
2589:             $name = "";
2590:         }
2591:         elsif (defined $command_definition)
2592:         {
2593:             $description = $command_definition->{description} ? "command $command_definition->{description}" : "(Warning: no description of this command_definition when ending the execution context)";
2594: 
2595:             $name = " ($command_definition->{command})";
2596:         }
2597:         else
2598:         {
2599:             $description = "module $module_definition->{description}";
2600: 
2601:             $name = " ($module_definition->{name})";
2602:         }
2603: 
2604:         if (defined $command_test)
2605:         {
2606:             # no output here, the test output is emitted in ->ec_examine()
2607:         }
2608:         else
2609:         {
2610:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message_end($fd_output, "End for tests of $description
2611: Total of $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{command_test} test(s) (encountered $global_error_count error(s) so far)");
2612:         }
2613:     }
2614: 
2615:     $self->_pop_current();
2616: }
2617: 
2618: 
2619: sub get_command_definition
2620: {
2621:     my $self = shift;
2622: 
2623:     my $current = $self->_get_current();
2624: 
2625:     my $command_definition = $current->[1];
2626: 
2627:     return $command_definition;
2628: }
2629: 
2630: 
2631: sub get_command_definition_counter_per_module
2632: {
2633:     my $self = shift;
2634: 
2635:     #t to be replaced with instance variables
2636: 
2637:     return $command_definition_counter_per_module;
2638: }
2639: 
2640: 
2641: sub get_command_test
2642: {
