2643:     my $self = shift;
2644: 
2645:     my $current = $self->_get_current();
2646: 
2647:     my $command_test = $current->[2];
2648: 
2649:     return $command_test;
2650: }
2651: 
2652: 
2653: sub get_command_test_counter_per_command_definition
2654: {
2655:     my $self = shift;
2656: 
2657:     #t to be replaced with instance variables
2658: 
2659:     return $command_test_counter_per_command_definition;
2660: }
2661: 
2662: 
2663: sub get_context_path
2664: {
2665:     my $self = shift;
2666: 
2667:     # add module counter and identifier
2668: 
2669:     my $result = $self->get_module_definition_counter();
2670: 
2671:     $result .= "_";
2672: 
2673:     $result .= $self->get_module_definition()->{name};
2674: 
2675:     # add command_definition counter and identifier
2676: 
2677:     my $command_definition = $self->get_command_definition();
2678: 
2679:     if (defined $command_definition)
2680:     {
2681:         $result .= "_";
2682: 
2683:         $result .= $self->get_command_definition_counter_per_module();
2684: 
2685:         $result .= "_";
2686: 
2687:         # the command_definition identifier is based on the system
2688:         # shell command that is used to perform the tests
2689: 
2690:         if (exists $command_definition->{command})
2691:         {
2692:             $result .= $command_definition->{command};
2693:         }
2694:         else
2695:         {
2696:             $result .= "NONE";
2697:         }
2698: 
2699:         # add command_test counter and identifier
2700: 
2701:         my $command_test = $self->get_command_test();
2702: 
2703:         if (defined $command_test)
2704:         {
2705:             $result .= "_";
2706: 
2707:             $result .= $self->get_command_test_counter_per_command_definition();
2708: 
2709:             $result .= "_";
2710: 
2711:             # the command_test identifier is based on the write clause
2712: 
2713:             if (exists $command_test->{write}
2714:                 and defined $command_test->{write})
2715:             {
2716:                 $result .= $command_test->{write};
2717:             }
2718:             else
2719:             {
2720:                 $result .= "NONE";
2721:             }
2722:         }
2723:     }
2724: 
2725:     # return result
2726: 
2727:     return $result;
2728: }
2729: 
2730: 
2731: sub get_module_definition
2732: {
2733:     my $self = shift;
2734: 
2735:     my $current = $self->_get_current();
2736: 
2737:     my $module_definition = $current->[0];
2738: 
2739:     return $module_definition;
2740: }
2741: 
2742: 
2743: sub get_module_definition_counter
2744: {
2745:     my $self = shift;
2746: 
2747:     #t to be replaced with instance variables
2748: 
2749:     return $module_counter_global;
2750: }
2751: 
2752: 
2753: sub has_error_flag
2754: {
2755:     my $self = shift;
2756: 
2757:     my $module_definition = shift;
2758: 
2759:     my $command_definition = shift;
2760: 
2761:     my $command_test = shift;
2762: 
2763:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
2764: 
2765:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
2766: 
2767:     my $active_element
2768:         = (defined $command_test
2769:            ? $command_test
2770:            : (defined $command_definition
2771:               ? $command_definition
2772:               : $module_definition));
2773: 
2774:     my $error = $active_element->{error};
2775: 
2776:     my $description = $active_element->{description};
2777: 
2778:     if ($error)
2779:     {
2780:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
2781:                 (
2782:                  $fd_output,
2783:                  {
2784:                   description => $description,
2785:                   error => "this test was tagged with the error flag",
2786:                   module_name => $module_name,
2787:                   subdescription => $error,
2788:                  },
2789:                 );
2790: 
2791:         return 1;
2792:     }
2793:     else
2794:     {
2795:         return 0;
2796:     }
2797: }
2798: 
2799: 
2800: sub is_disabled
2801: {
2802:     my $self = shift;
2803: 
2804:     my $module_definition = shift;
2805: 
2806:     my $command_definition = shift;
2807: 
2808:     my $command_test = shift;
2809: 
2810:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
2811: 
2812:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
2813: 
2814:     my $active_element
2815:         = (defined $command_test
2816:            ? $command_test
2817:            : (defined $command_definition
2818:               ? $command_definition
2819:               : $module_definition));
2820: 
2821:     my $disabled = $active_element->{disabled};
2822: 
2823:     if ($disabled)
2824:     {
2825:         my $description = $active_element->{description};
2826: 
2827:         my $report_key
2828:             = (defined $command_test
2829:                ? "command_tests"
2830:                : (defined $command_definition
2831:                   ? "command_definitions"
2832:                   : "modules"));
2833: 
2834:         $global_test_report->{disabled}->{$report_key}->{$module_name}->{$description} = $disabled;
2835: 
2836:         if ($global_test_report->{output_format} eq 'executor')
2837:         {
2838:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message_info($fd_output, "Tests of $description are disabled ($disabled)
2839: Total of $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{command_test} test(s) (encountered $global_error_count error(s) so far)");
2840:         }
2841: 
2842:         return 1;
2843:     }
2844:     else
2845:     {
2846:         return 0;
2847:     }
2848: }
2849: 
2850: 
2851: sub is_selected
2852: {
2853:     my $self = shift;
2854: 
2855:     my $module_definition = shift;
2856: 
2857:     my $command_definition = shift;
2858: 
2859:     my $command_test = shift;
2860: 
2861:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
2862: 
2863:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
2864: 
2865:     my $active_element
2866:         = (defined $command_test
2867:            ? $command_test
2868:            : (defined $command_definition
2869:               ? $command_definition
2870:               : $module_definition));
2871: 
2872:     my $result = $active_element->is_selected($fd_output);
2873: 
2874:     return $result;
2875: }
2876: 
2877: 
2878: sub _preparation
2879: {
2880:     my $self = shift;
2881: 
2882:     my $current = $self->_get_current();
2883: 
2884:     my $module_definition = $current->[0];
2885: 
2886:     my $command_definition = $current->[1];
2887: 
2888:     my $command_test = $current->[2];
2889: 
2890:     # command_tests with a harnessing clause are unsupported
2891: 
2892:     if ($command_test)
2893:     {
2894:         return undef;
2895:     }
2896: 
2897:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
2898: 
2899:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
2900: 
2901:     if ($command_definition)
2902:     {
2903:         if (exists $command_definition->{preparation}
2904:             and exists $command_definition->{harnessing})
2905:         {
2906:             die "$0: command_definition ($command_definition->{description}) has both a preparation and harnessing clause\n";
2907:         }
2908:     }
2909:     else
2910:     {
2911:         if (exists $module_definition->{preparation}
2912:             and exists $module_definition->{harnessing})
2913:         {
2914:             die "$0: module_definition ($module_definition->{description}) has both a preparation and harnessing clause\n";
2915:         }
2916:     }
2917: 
2918:     my $active_element
2919:         = (defined $command_test
2920:            ? $command_test
2921:            : (defined $command_definition
2922:               ? $command_definition
2923:               : $module_definition));
2924: 
2925:     # convert the old format preparation / reparation to the new format of harnessing
2926: 
2927:     if (not exists $active_element->{harnessing})
2928:     {
2929:         if (exists $active_element->{preparation})
2930:         {
2931:             $active_element->{harnessing}->{preparation} = $active_element->{preparation};
2932: 
2933:             delete $active_element->{preparation};
2934:         }
2935: 
2936:         if (exists $active_element->{reparation})
2937:         {
2938:             $active_element->{harnessing}->{reparation} = $active_element->{reparation};
2939: 
2940:             delete $active_element->{reparation};
2941:         }
2942:     }
2943: 
2944:     # process the harnessing preparation clause
2945: 
2946:     my $harnessing = $active_element->{harnessing};
2947: 
2948:     my $preparation = $harnessing->{preparation};
2949: 
2950:     my $preparation_result;
2951: 
2952:     # if we are really running tests and there is a preparation clause
2953: 
2954:     if (($global_test_report->{output_format} eq 'executor')
2955:         and $preparation)
2956:     {
2957:         # give diagnostics
2958: 
2959:         my $preparation_name
2960:             = ($command_definition
2961:                ? "command '$command_definition->{command}'"
2962:                : "module '$module_definition->{name}'");
2963: 
2964:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message($fd_output, "*** Preparing $preparation_name ($preparation->{description})");
2965: 
2966:         # execute the preparation clause
2967: 
2968:         my $preparer = $preparation->{preparer};
2969: 
2970:         $preparation_result = _prepre_engine($preparer, 'preparation');
2971: 
2972:         # if the preparer returned an error string
2973: 
2974:         my $preparation_error;
2975: 
2976:         if (ref $preparation_result eq 'SCALAR')
2977:         {
2978:             # obtain the error string
2979: 
2980:             $preparation_error = $$preparation_result;
2981:         }
2982: 
2983:         # process errors
2984: 
2985:         if ($preparation_error)
2986:         {
2987:             my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
2988: 
2989:             my $subdescription
2990:                 = ($command_definition
2991:                    ? $command_definition->{description}
2992:                    : $module_definition->{description});
2993: 
2994:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
2995:                 (
2996:                  $fd_output,
2997:                  {
2998:                      description => $preparation_name,
2999:                      error => $preparation_error,
3000:                      module_name => $module_name,
3001:                      subdescription => $subdescription,
3002:                  },
3003:                 );
3004:         }
3005:     }
3006: 
3007:     # return result
3008: 
3009:     return $preparation_result;
3010: }
3011: 
3012: 
3013: sub new
3014: {
3015:     my $package = shift;
3016: 
3017:     my $fd_output = shift;
3018: 
3019:     my $options = shift || {};
3020: 
3021:     my $self
3022:         = {
3023:            %$options,
3024:            fd_output => $fd_output,
3025:            path => [],
3026:            preparation_result => [],
3027:           };
3028: 
3029:     bless $self, $package;
3030: 
3031:     return $self;
3032: }
3033: 
3034: 
3035: sub _reparation
3036: {
3037:     my $self = shift;
3038: 
3039:     my $current = $self->_get_current();
3040: 
3041:     my $module_definition = $current->[0];
3042: 
3043:     my $command_definition = $current->[1];
3044: 
3045:     my $command_test = $current->[2];
3046: 
3047:     my $preparation_result = $self->_pop_preparation_result();
3048: 
3049:     # command_tests with a harnessing clause are unsupported
3050: 
3051:     if ($command_test)
3052:     {
3053:         return undef;
3054:     }
3055: 
3056:     my $active_element
3057:         = (defined $command_test
3058:            ? $command_test
3059:            : (defined $command_definition
3060:               ? $command_definition
3061:               : $module_definition));
3062: 
3063:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
3064: 
3065:     # process the harnessing reparation clause
3066: 
3067:     my $harnessing
3068:         = ($command_definition
3069:            ? $command_definition->{harnessing}
3070:            : $module_definition->{harnessing});
3071: 
3072:     my $reparation = $harnessing->{reparation};
3073: 
3074:     my $reparation_error;
3075: 
3076:     # if there is a reparation clause
3077: 
3078:     if (($global_test_report->{output_format} eq 'executor')
3079:         and $reparation)
3080:     {
3081:         # give diagnostics
3082: 
3083:         my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
3084: 
3085:         my $reparation_description = $reparation->{description};
3086: 
3087:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message($fd_output, "*** Reparing ($reparation_description)");
3088: 
3089:         # execute the reparation clause
3090: 
3091:         my $reparer = $reparation->{reparer};
3092: 
3093:         $reparation_error = _prepre_engine($reparer, 'reparation', $preparation_result);
3094: 
3095:         # process errors
3096: 
3097:         if ($reparation_error)
3098:         {
3099:             my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
3100: 
3101:             my $subdescription
3102:                 = ($command_definition
3103:                    ? $command_definition->{description}
3104:                    : $module_definition->{description});
3105: 
3106:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
3107:                 (
3108:                  $fd_output,
3109:                  {
3110:                      description => $reparation_description,
3111:                      error => $reparation_error,
3112:                      module_name => $module_name,
3113:                      subdescription => $subdescription,
3114:                  },
3115:                 );
3116:         }
3117:     }
3118: 
3119:     return $reparation_error;
3120: }
3121: 
3122: 
3123: #
3124: # sub start
3125: #
3126: # Apply selection criteria to the current context.
3127: # Prepare the test environment.
3128: #
3129: 
3130: sub ec_examine
3131: {
3132:     my $self = shift;
3133: 
3134:     my $module_definition = shift;
3135: 
3136:     my $command_definition = shift;
3137: 
3138:     my $command_test = shift;
3139: 
3140:     # set default result: everything ok
3141: 
3142:     my $result;
3143: 
3144:     # if this element is selected by active options
3145: 
3146:     if (not $self->is_selected($module_definition, $command_definition, $command_test))
3147:     {
3148:         return "not selected";
3149:     }
3150: 
3151:     # if this test is disabled
3152: 
3153:     if ($self->is_disabled($module_definition, $command_definition, $command_test))
3154:     {
3155:         # return to skip it
3156: 
3157:         return "is disbled";
3158:     }
3159: 
3160:     # if this test has been flagged with an error
3161: 
3162:     if ($self->has_error_flag($module_definition, $command_definition, $command_test))
3163:     {
3164:         # return to skip it
3165: 
3166:         return "has the error flag";;
3167:     }
3168: 
3169:     # update the path of execution
3170: 
3171:     $self->_push_current( [ $module_definition, $command_definition, $command_test, ] );
3172: 
3173:     # give diagnostics
3174: 
3175:     if ($global_test_report->{output_format} eq 'executor')
3176:     {
3177:         my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
3178: 
3179:         my $comment;
3180: 
3181:         my $description;
3182: 
3183:         my $name;
3184: 
3185:         if (defined $command_test)
3186:         {
3187:             $comment = $command_test->{comment};
3188: 
3189:             $description = $command_test->{description} ? "*** Test: $command_test->{description}" : "(Warning: no description of this command_test when examining the execution context)";
3190: 
3191:             $name = "";
3192:         }
3193:         elsif (defined $command_definition)
3194:         {
3195:             $comment = $command_definition->{comment};
3196: 
3197:             $description = $command_definition->{description} ? "command $command_definition->{description}" : "(Warning: no description of this command_definition when examining the execution context)";
3198: 
3199:             $name = " ($command_definition->{command})";
3200:         }
3201:         else
3202:         {
3203:             $comment = $module_definition->{comment};
3204: 
3205:             $description = "module $module_definition->{description}";
3206: 
3207:             $name = " ($module_definition->{name})";
3208:         }
3209: 
3210:         if (defined $command_test)
3211:         {
3212:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message($fd_output, "$description");
3213:         }
3214:         else
3215:         {
3216:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message_start($fd_output, "Running tests of $description$name");
3217:         }
3218: 
3219:         if ($comment)
3220:         {
3221:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message($fd_output, "*** Comment: $comment");
3222:         }
3223:     }
3224: 
3225:     # prepare the test environment
3226: 
3227:     my $preparation_result = $self->_preparation();
3228: 
3229:     $self->_push_preparation_result($preparation_result);
3230: 
3231:     # return result: should be undef here
3232: 
3233:     return $result;
3234: }
3235: 
3236: 
3237: sub terminate
3238: {
3239:     my $self = shift;
3240: 
3241:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
3242: 
3243:     my $path = $self->{path};
3244: 
3245:     if (scalar @$path)
3246:     {
3247:         my $module_definition = $self->get_module_definition();
3248: 
3249:         my $description = "module $module_definition->{description}";
3250: 
3251:         my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
3252: 
3253:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
3254:             (
3255:              $fd_output,
3256:              {
3257:               description => "execution context invalid",
3258:               error => "left over elements in the execution context after it was terminated",
3259:               module_name => $module_name,
3260:              },
3261:             );
3262:     }
3263: }
3264: 
3265: 
3266: sub wait
3267: {
3268:     my $self = shift;
3269: 
3270:     my $fd_output = shift;
3271: 
3272:     my $wait = shift;
3273: 
3274:     # wait
3275: 
3276:     if ($wait)
3277:     {
3278:         print $fd_output "*** Wait: $wait seconds\n";
3279: 
3280:         select(undef, undef, undef, $wait);
3281:     }
3282: }
3283: 
3284: 
3285: package Heterarch::Test::Executor;
3286: 
3287: #
3288: # This is the default Heterarch::Test::Executor.  It provides a link
3289: # with the execution context and implements ->ex_mo_prepare(),
3290: # ->ex_cd_start() and ->ex_ct_run() methods that call the appropriate
3291: # module, command_definition and command_test methods for implementing
3292: # test execution.
3293: #
3294: 
3295: 
3296: sub new
3297: {
3298:     my $package = shift;
3299: 
3300:     my $execution_context = shift;
3301: 
3302:     my $fd_output = $execution_context->{fd_output};
3303: 
3304:     my $options = shift || {};
3305: 
3306:     my $self
3307:         = {
3308:            %$options,
3309:            execution_context => $execution_context,
3310:            fd_output => $fd_output,
3311:           };
3312: 
3313:     bless $self, $package;
3314: 
3315:     return $self;
3316: }
3317: 
3318: 
3319: #
3320: # Create the infrastructure for keeping module test results.
3321: #
3322: # For a base executor, this is currently a no-op.
3323: #
3324: # There is currently a global infrastructure for storing real test
3325: # results, rather than a local one.
3326: #
3327: 
3328: sub ex_mo_prepare
3329: {
3330:     my $self = shift;
3331: 
3332: }
3333: 
3334: 
3335: #
3336: # ->run() is called to execute one command test.
3337: #
3338: # this executor trivially delegates this call to the command_test object
3339: #
3340: # A derived class can inspect the elements found in the test
3341: # specification and, for instance, print them to a file.
3342: #
3343: 
3344: sub ex_ct_run
3345: {
3346:     my $self = shift;
3347: 
3348:     my $command_test = shift;
3349: 
3350:     my $execution_context = $self->{execution_context};
3351: 
3352:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
3353: 
3354:     return $command_test->ct_run($execution_context, $self, $fd_output);
3355: }
3356: 
3357: 
3358: #
3359: # ->start() is called to start the tests of a single command definition.
3360: #
3361: # It instantiate the tester:
3362: #
3363: # 1. an object given its class.
3364: # 2. a piece of perl code that is run.
3365: # 3. an interactive system shell command.
3366: #
3367: 
3368: sub ex_cd_start
3369: {
3370:     my $self = shift;
3371: 
3372:     my $execution_context = $self->{execution_context};
3373: 
3374:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
3375: 
3376:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
3377: 
3378:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
3379: 
3380:     $command_definition->cd_start($self, $fd_output);
3381: 
3382:     # process the command_definition specific clauses
3383: 
3384:     #! this sloppy implementation works around a too elaborate
3385:     #! object-oriented based implementation that would require
3386:     #! to call SUPER class methods inside ->cd_start().
3387: 
3388:     $command_definition->process_clauses($execution_context, $fd_output);
3389: }
3390: 
3391: 
3392: sub ex_terminate
3393: {
3394:     my $self = shift;
3395: 
3396:     my $fd_output = shift;
3397: 
3398:     my $test_module_library_contents = shift;
3399: 
3400:     # yaml out the test report
3401: 
3402:     my $report_yaml
3403:         = {
3404:            description => $global_test_report->{description},
3405:           };
3406: 
3407:     # add output specific to the output selection
3408: 
3409:     if ($option_report_disabled)
3410:     {
3411:         $report_yaml->{disabled} = $global_test_report->{disabled};
3412:     }
3413: 
3414:     $report_yaml->{errors} = $global_test_report->{errors};
3415:     $report_yaml->{global} = $global_test_report->{global};
3416: 
3417:     use YAML;
3418: 
3419:     my $report_text = "\n" . Dump($report_yaml);
3420: 
3421:     print $global_fd_output "$report_text\n";
3422: 
3423:     #! this could be an error message
3424: 
3425:     return undef;
3426: }
3427: 
3428: 
3429: package Heterarch::Test::Library;
3430: 
3431: 
3432: sub construct
3433: {
3434:     # find the test specifications
3435: 
3436:     my $test_modules
3437:         = [
3438:            map
3439:            {
3440:                chomp; $_;
3441:            }
3442:            sort
3443:            `find $global_config->{tests_directory} -name "*.t"`,
3444:           ];
3445: 
3446:     return $test_modules;
3447: }
3448: 
3449: 
3450: sub expand
3451: {
3452:     my $fd_output = shift;;
3453: 
3454:     my $library = shift;
3455: 
3456:     my $test_modules = [];
3457: 
3458:     # read the introduction
3459: 
3460:     my $html_introduction;
3461: 
3462:     {
3463:         local $/; # enable 'slurp' mode
3464: 
3465:         my $file = IO::File->new("<$global_config->{introduction_filename}");
3466: 
3467:         $html_introduction = <$file>;
3468: 
3469:         $file->close();
3470:     }
3471: 
3472:     # parse all modules
3473: 
3474:     foreach my $test_module (@$library)
3475:     {
3476:         if ($test_module !~ /$option_regex_selector/i)
3477:         {
3478:             next;
3479:         }
3480: 
3481:         my $module_definition = readfile($fd_output, $test_module);
3482: 
3483:         if ($module_definition)
3484:         {
3485:             push @$test_modules, $module_definition;
3486:         }
3487:     }
3488: 
3489:     if ($option_flattenout)
3490:     {
3491:         # sort modules
3492: 
3493:         #t first need to transform: select command_definitions, flatten
3494:         #t out, keep the module names (for referencing errors).
3495: 
3496:         my $transformator
3497:             = Data::Transformator->new
3498:                 (
3499:                  apply_identity_transformation => 0,
3500:                  name => 'test-module-selector',
3501:                  contents => $test_modules,
3502:                  separator => '`',
3503:                  array_filter =>
3504:                  sub
3505:                  {
3506:                      my ($context, $component) = @_;
3507: 
3508:                      # never filter for the first two component in the path
3509: 
3510:                      my $depth = $context->{array};
3511:                      $depth = $#$depth;
3512: 
3513:                      if ($depth < 2)
3514:                      {
3515:                          return 1;
3516:                      }
3517: 
3518:                      # extract the data: command definitions with test commands
3519: 
3520:                      $context->{path} =~ m|^[^/]*/([^/]*)/([^/]*)|;
3521: 
3522:                      my $content = Data::Transformator::_context_get_current_content($context);
3523: 
3524:                      # push it onto the result
3525: 
3526:                      my $result = Data::Transformator::_context_get_main_result($context);
3527: 
3528:                      if (!$result->{content})
3529:                      {
3530:                          $result->{content} = [];
3531:                      }
3532: 
3533:                      push @{$result->{content}}, $content;
3534: 
3535:                      # add the module name
3536: 
3537:                      my $module_name = $context->{array}->[1]->{content}->{name};
3538: 
3539:                      $content->{module_name} = $module_name;
3540: 
3541:                      # add the module description
3542: 
3543:                      my $module_description = $context->{array}->[1]->{content}->{description};
3544: 
3545:                      $content->{module_description} = $module_description;
3546: 
3547:                      # result is known, everything gets filtered
3548: 
3549:                      0;
3550:                  },
3551:                 );
3552: 
3553:         #t for an empty array as content, the transformator returns an
3554:         #t undef, this is a bug that still needs fixing.
3555: 
3556:         my $tests = $transformator->transform() || [];
3557: 
3558:         # sort the flattened test definitions
3559: 
3560:         $tests
3561:             = [
3562:                sort
3563:                {
3564:                    my $module1 = $a;
3565:                    my $module2 = $b;
3566: 
3567:                    my $command1 = $module1->{command};
3568:                    my $command2 = $module2->{command};
3569: 
3570:                    my $command1_arguments = $module1->{arguments} || [];
3571:                    my $command2_arguments = $module2->{arguments} || [];
3572: 
3573:                    my $command1_string = join ' ', $command1, @$command1_arguments;
3574:                    my $command2_string = join ' ', $command2, @$command2_arguments;
3575: 
3576:                    my $comparison = $command1_string cmp $command2_string;
3577: 
3578:                    if ($module1->{tester_head})
3579:                    {
3580:                        $comparison = -1;
3581:                    }
3582:                    elsif ($module2->{tester_head})
3583:                    {
3584:                        $comparison = 1;
3585:                    }
3586: 
3587:                    $comparison;
3588:                }
3589:                @$tests,
3590:               ];
3591: 
3592:         # transform back to the regular test module format by putting
3593:         # every command definition in its own module
3594: 
3595:         $tests
3596:             = [
3597:                map
3598:                {
3599:                    my $command_definition = $_;
3600: 
3601:                    (
3602:                     {
3603:                      command_definitions => [
3604:                                              $command_definition,
3605:                                             ],
3606:                      description => $command_definition->{module_description},
3607:                      name => $command_definition->{module_name},
3608:                     }
3609:                    );
3610:                }
3611:                @$tests,
3612:               ];
3613: 
3614:         # set result
3615: 
3616:         $test_modules = $tests;
3617:     }
3618: 
3619:     # set result
3620: 
3621:     my $result
3622:         = {
3623:            html_introduction => $html_introduction,
3624:            test_modules => $test_modules,
3625:           };
3626: 
3627:     # return result
3628: 
3629:     return $result;
3630: }
3631: 
3632: 
3633: sub is_json
3634: {
3635:     my $filename = shift;
3636: 
3637:     my $result;
3638: 
3639:     use IO::File;
3640: 
3641:     my $fh = IO::File->new();
3642: 
3643:     if ($fh->open("< $filename"))
3644:     {
3645:         my $first_line = <$fh>;
3646: 
3647:         $first_line =~ s/(\s)*//g;
3648: 
3649:         if ($first_line =~ m'^\{')
3650:         {
3651:             $result = 'is_json';
3652:         }
3653: 
3654:         $fh->close;
3655:     }
3656: 
3657:     return $result;
3658: }
3659: 
3660: 
3661: sub is_yaml
3662: {
3663:     my $filename = shift;
3664: 
3665:     my $result;
3666: 
3667:     use IO::File;
3668: 
3669:     my $fh = IO::File->new();
3670: 
3671:     if ($fh->open("< $filename"))
3672:     {
3673:         my $first_line = <$fh>;
3674: 
3675:         $first_line =~ s/(\s)*//g;
3676: 
3677:         if ($first_line eq '---')
3678:         {
3679:             $result = 'is_yaml';
3680:         }
3681: 
3682:         $fh->close;
3683:     }
3684: 
3685:     return $result;
3686: }
3687: 
3688: 
3689: sub randomize_order
3690: {
3691:     my $test_modules = shift;
3692: 
3693:     my $random_seed = shift;
3694: 
3695:     # by default the randomizer will choose a random_seed
3696: 
3697:     if (not defined $random_seed)
3698:     {
3699:         $random_seed = time() ^ $$ ^ unpack "%L*", `ps axww | gzip`;
3700:     }
3701: 
3702:     # set the random_seed
3703: 
3704:     srand($random_seed);
3705: 
3706:     # and shuffle the modules
3707: 
3708:     require List::Util;
3709: 
3710:     return ( [ List::Util::shuffle(@$test_modules), ], $random_seed );
3711: }
3712: 
3713: 
3714: sub readfile
3715: {
3716:     my $fd_output = shift;
3717: 
3718:     my $pathname = shift;
3719: 
3720:     my $result;
3721: 
3722:     if (is_yaml($pathname))
3723:     {
3724:         use YAML;
3725: 
3726:         $result = YAML::LoadFile($pathname);
3727: 
3728:         if ($@)
3729:         {
3730:             report_error_add
3731:             (
3732:              $fd_output,
3733:              {
3734:               description => $@,
3735:               error => $@,
3736:               module_name => $pathname,
3737:              },
3738:             );
3739:         }
3740:     }
3741:     elsif (is_json($pathname))
3742:     {
3743:         local $/; # enable 'slurp' mode
3744: 
3745:         my $file = IO::File->new("<$pathname");
3746: 
3747:         my $json_text = <$file>;
3748: 
3749:         $file->close();
3750: 
3751:         use JSON;
3752: 
3753:         my $json = JSON->new();
3754: 
3755:         #! allow code refs, they are converted to nulls
3756: 
3757:         $json->allow_unknown(1);
3758: 
3759:         $result = $json->decode($json_text);
3760: 
3761:         if ($@)
3762:         {
3763:             report_error_add
3764:             (
3765:              $fd_output,
3766:              {
3767:               description => $@,
3768:               error => $@,
3769:               module_name => $pathname,
3770:              },
3771:             );
3772:         }
3773:     }
3774:     else
3775:     {
3776:         $result = do $pathname;
3777: 
3778:         if ($@)
3779:         {
3780:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
3781:                     (
3782:                      $fd_output,
3783:                      {
3784:                       description => $@,
3785:                       error => $@,
3786:                       module_name => $pathname,
3787:                      },
3788:                     );
3789:         }
3790:     }
3791: 
3792:     $pathname =~ m((.*)/(.*));
3793: 
3794:     my $filename = $2;
3795: 
3796:     if ($option_dump_json)
3797:     {
3798:         use JSON;
3799: 
3800:         my $json = JSON->new();
3801: 
3802:         #! allow code refs, they are converted to nulls
3803: 
3804:         $json->allow_unknown(1);
3805: 
3806:         my $file = IO::File->new(">/tmp/$filename.js");
3807: 
3808:         print $file $json->encode($result);
3809: 
3810:         $file->close();
3811:     }
3812: 
3813:     if ($option_dump_perl)
3814:     {
3815:         use Data::Dumper;
3816: 
3817:         my $file = IO::File->new(">/tmp/$filename.pl");
3818: 
3819:         print $file Dumper($result);
3820: 
3821:         $file->close();
3822:     }
3823: 
3824:     if ($option_dump_yaml)
3825:     {
3826:         YAML::DumpFile("/tmp/$filename.yml", $result);
3827:     }
3828: 
3829:     # check if the name of the test and the pathname match
3830: 
3831:     if ($option_check_test_names)
3832:     {
3833:         if ($pathname ne "$global_config->{tests_directory}/$result->{name}")
3834:         {
3835:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
3836:                 (
3837:                  $fd_output,
3838:                  {
3839:                      description => "the pathname ($pathname) and the test name ($result->{name}) are different",
3840:                      error => "the pathname ($pathname) and the test name ($result->{name}) are different",
3841:                      module_name => $pathname,
3842:                  },
3843:                 );
3844:         }
3845:     }
3846: 
3847:     return $result;
3848: }
3849: 
3850: 
3851: package Heterarch::Test::Module;
3852: 
3853: 
3854: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Element");
3855: 
3856: 
3857: # could also use Array::Util, but it is not always installed
3858: 
3859: sub array_union_intersection_difference
3860: {
3861:     my $array1 = shift;
3862: 
3863:     my $array2 = shift;
3864: 
3865:     my (@union, @intersection, @difference);
3866: 
3867:     my %count = ();
3868: 
3869:     foreach my $element (@$array1, @$array2)
3870:     {
3871:         $count{$element}++
3872:     }
3873: 
3874:     foreach my $element (keys %count)
3875:     {
3876:         push @union, $element;
3877: 
3878:         push @{ $count{$element} > 1
3879:                     ? \@intersection
3880:                     : \@difference },
3881:                     $element;
3882:     }
3883: 
3884:     return ( \@union, \@intersection, \@difference );
3885: }
3886: 
3887: 
3888: sub check_description
3889: {
3890:     my $module_definition = shift;
3891: 
3892:     my $fd_output = shift;
3893: 
3894:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
3895: 
3896:     if (!defined $module_definition->{description})
3897:     {
3898:         $module_definition->{description} = $module_definition->{name};
3899: 
3900:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
3901:                 (
3902:                  $fd_output,
3903:                  {
3904:                   description => 'no module description',
3905:                   error => 'no module description',
3906:                   module_name => $module_name,
3907:                  },
3908:                 );
3909:     }
3910: 
3911:     return $module_definition->{description};
3912: }
3913: 
3914: 
3915: sub check_name
3916: {
3917:     my $module_definition = shift;
3918: 
3919:     my $fd_output = shift;
3920: 
3921:     if (!defined $module_definition->{name})
3922:     {
3923:         $module_definition->{name} = 'unnamed';
3924: 
3925:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
3926:                 (
3927:                  $fd_output,
3928:                  {
3929:                   description => 'unnamed module',
3930:                   error => 'unnamed module',
3931:                   module_name => 'unnamed module',
3932:                  },
3933:                 );
3934:     }
3935: 
3936:     return $module_definition->{name};
3937: }
3938: 
3939: 
3940: sub is_selected
3941: {
3942:     my $self = shift;
3943: 
3944:     my $fd_output = shift;
3945: 
3946:     # implement a logical and of the different selection criteria
3947: 
3948:     my $result = $self->SUPER::is_selected($fd_output);
3949: 
3950:     if ($result)
3951:     {
3952:         $result = $self->is_selected_by_tag($fd_output);
3953:     }
3954: 
3955:     return $result;
3956: }
3957: 
3958: 
3959: # return true if this module definition has a tag that matches the tag options.
3960: 
3961: sub is_selected_by_tag
3962: {
3963:     my $module_definition = shift;
3964: 
3965:     my $fd_output = shift;
3966: 
3967:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
3968: 
3969:     if (not scalar @$option_tags)
3970:     {
3971:         return ":all";
3972:     }
3973: 
3974:     my $module_definition_tags = $module_definition->{tags};
3975: 
3976:     # use Array::Util qw(intersect);
3977: 
3978:     my ( $union, $intersection, $difference ) = array_union_intersection_difference($module_definition_tags, $option_tags);
3979: 
3980:     if (scalar @$intersection)
3981:     {
3982:         return $intersection;
3983:     }
3984:     else
3985:     {
3986:         if ($global_test_report->{output_format} eq 'executor')
3987:         {
3988:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_message_info($fd_output, "Module $module_definition->{description} is not selected by tag (" . (join ", ", @$option_tags ) . " are selected tags).
3989: Total of $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{command_test} test(s) (encountered $global_error_count error(s) so far)");
3990:         }
3991: 
3992:         $global_test_report->{not_selected_by_tag}->{modules}->{$module_name} = 'not_selected_by_tag';
3993: 
3994:         return undef;
3995:     }
3996: }
3997: 
3998: 
3999: # check if this module is ready to run its tests.
4000: # execute all the command definitions.
4001: # execute all the command tests in these command definitions.
4002: 
4003: sub md_run
4004: {
4005:     my $self = shift;
4006: 
4007:     my $execution_context = shift;
4008: 
4009:     my $executor = shift;
4010: 
4011:     my $fd_output = shift;
4012: 
4013:     my $module_name = $self->check_name($fd_output);
4014: 
4015:     my $module_description = $self->check_description($fd_output);
4016: 
4017:     # do selection and prepare the test environment
4018: 
4019:     my $not_started = $execution_context->ec_examine($self);
4020: 
4021:     if ($not_started)
4022:     {
4023:         return "module start failed ($not_started)";
4024:     }
4025: 
4026:     # create the infrastructure for keeping module test results
4027: 
4028:     $executor->ex_mo_prepare();
4029: 
4030:     # loop over commands for this module
4031: 
4032:     my $command_definitions = $self->{command_definitions};
4033: 
4034:     foreach my $command_definition (@$command_definitions)
4035:     {
4036:         bless $command_definition, "Heterarch::Test::CommandDefinition";
4037: 
4038:         # lookup the class for this command definition
4039: 
4040:         $command_definition->rebless();
4041: 
4042:         # do selection and prepare the test environment
4043: 
4044:         my $not_started = $execution_context->ec_examine($self, $command_definition);
4045: 
4046:         if ($not_started)
4047:         {
4048:             next;
4049:         }
4050: 
4051:         # call the perl code, spaw_new
4052: 
4053:         # 1. an object given its class.
4054:         # 2. a piece of perl code that is run.
4055:         # 3. an interactive system shell command.
4056: 
4057:         $executor->ex_cd_start();
4058: 
4059:         # loop over all tests for this command
4060: 
4061:         my $command_tests = $command_definition->{command_tests};
4062: 
4063:         foreach my $command_test (@$command_tests)
4064:         {
4065:             bless $command_test, "Heterarch::Test::CommandTest";
4066: 
4067:             # lookup the class for this command test
4068: 
4069:             $command_test->rebless();
4070: 
4071:             # emit comment if any
4072: 
4073:             my $not_started = $execution_context->ec_examine($self, $command_definition, $command_test);
4074: 
4075:             if ($not_started)
4076:             {
4077:                 next;
4078:             }
4079: 
4080:             # run the text executor which will run the command_test
4081: 
4082:             my $execution_result = $executor->ex_ct_run($command_test);
4083: 
4084:             # process errors
4085: 
4086:             my $error = $execution_result->{error};
4087: 
4088:             if ($error)
4089:             {
4090:                 my $description = $command_test->{description};
4091: 
4092:                 my $command_definition_description = $command_definition->{description};
4093: 
4094:                 my $message;
4095: 
4096:                 my $before_match = $execution_result->{before_match};
4097: 
4098:                 #! wondering what option_verbose has to do with the reporting of errors
4099: 
4100:                 if ($option_verbose)
4101:                 {
4102:                     $message = $before_match;
4103:                 }
4104: 
4105:                 Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
4106:                         (
4107:                          $fd_output,
4108:                          {
4109:                           description => $description,
4110:                           error => $error,
4111:                           expected => $execution_result->{expected},
4112:                           message => $message,
4113:                           module_name => $module_name,
4114:                           seen => $before_match,
4115:                           subdescription => $command_definition_description,
4116:                          },
4117:                         );
4118:             }
4119: 
4120:             # end the execution of this test
4121: 
4122:             $execution_context->ec_end($self, $command_definition, $command_test);
4123: 
4124:             # register if this command had side effects
4125: 
4126:             $global_previous_command_side_effects ||= $command_test->{side_effects} || 0;
4127: 
4128:             # increment command test count
4129: 
4130:             $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{command_test}++;
4131:         }
4132: 
4133:         # repair the command test environment
4134: 
4135:         $execution_context->ec_end($self, $command_definition);
4136: 
4137:         # register if this command had side effects
4138: 
4139:         #! e.g. using a preparer/reparer combination
4140: 
4141:         $global_previous_command_side_effects ||= $command_definition->{side_effects} || 0;
4142: 
4143:         # increment command definition test count
4144: 
4145:         $global_test_report->{global}->{test_counters}->{command_definition}++;
4146:     }
4147: 
4148:     # repair the test environment
4149: 
4150:     $execution_context->ec_end($self);
4151: 
4152:     # register if this command had side effects
4153: 
4154:     #! don't think this make sense, but anyway ...
4155: 
4156:     $global_previous_command_side_effects ||= $self->{side_effects} || 0;
4157: 
4158:     # if library checksum mismatch
4159: 
4160:     if (defined ($global_config->{model_library}))
4161:     {
4162:         my $library_sha_before = $execution_context->{library_sha_before};
4163: 
4164:         my $library_sha_after = ModelLibrary::sha();
4165: 
4166:         if ($library_sha_after ne $library_sha_before)
4167:         {
4168:             my $error = 'model library checksum mismatch (model library has changed)';
4169: 
4170:             my $description = $self->{description};
4171: 
4172:             Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
4173:                     (
4174:                      $fd_output,
4175:                      {
4176:                       description => $description,
4177:                       error => $error,
4178:                       module_name => $module_name,
4179:                      },
4180:                     );
4181:         }
4182:     }
4183: 
4184:     # return result: execution completed
4185: 
4186:     return '';
4187: }
4188: 
4189: 
4190: package Heterarch::Test::Output;
4191: 
4192: 
4193: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Executor");
4194: 
4195: 
4196: package Heterarch::Test::Output::Summarizer;
4197: 
4198: #
4199: # A Heterarch::Test::Output::Summarizer implements ->ex_mo_prepare(),
4200: # ex_cd_start() and ex_ct_run() methods that select content and
4201: # prepare a structure for generating a summary in YAML of this
4202: # selection.
4203: #
4204: 
4205: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Output");
4206: 
4207: 
4208: package Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter;
4209: 
4210: #
4211: # A Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter implements ->ex_mo_prepare(),
4212: # ->ex_cd_start() and ->ex_ct_run() methods that prepare structure
4213: # content for the output formatter to convert to its target format.
4214: #
4215: # The result of the conversion is stored in the hash
4216: # $global_test_report->{selected}.
4217: #
4218: # This hash has the selected module names as keys.  Each value is a
4219: # hash with the contents of the module and a key
4220: # ->{command_definitions} with as value an array for the contents of
4221: # the command_definitions.  Each command_definition value has again
4222: # the content of a command_definition and a key ->{command_tests}.
4223: #
4224: # Each element in the result receives a key ->{context_path} that is
4225: # unique and identifies the path from the root to the element.
4226: #
4227: 
4228: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Output");
4229: 
4230: 
4231: #
4232: # Create the infrastructure for keeping module test results.
4233: #
4234: 
4235: sub ex_mo_prepare
4236: {
4237:     my $self = shift;
4238: 
4239:     # create an output entry for the module
4240: 
4241:     my $execution_context = $self->{execution_context};
4242: 
4243:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
4244: 
4245:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
4246: 
4247:     if (not $global_test_report->{selected}->{$module_name})
4248:     {
4249:         my $module_counter = $execution_context->get_module_definition_counter();
4250: 
4251:         my $context_path = $execution_context->get_context_path();;
4252: 
4253:         if ($option_verbose)
4254:         {
4255:             print $global_fd_output "$0: Preparing $context_path\n";
4256:         }
4257: 
4258:         $global_test_report->{selected}->{$module_name}
4259:             = {
4260:                command_definitions => [],
4261:                comment => $module_definition->{comment},
4262:                context_path => $context_path,
4263:                count => $module_counter,
4264:                description => $module_definition->{description},
4265:                documentation => $module_definition->{documentation},
4266:                harnessing => {
4267:                               preparation => $module_definition->{harnessing}->{preparation}->{description},
4268:                               reparation => $module_definition->{harnessing}->{reparation}->{description},
4269:                              },
4270:                name => $module_name,
4271:               };
4272:     }
4273:     else
4274:     {
4275:         die "$0: *** Error: module_name $module_name is defined twice.";
4276:     }
4277: }
4278: 
4279: 
4280: #
4281: # Adds the current test description to the summary, constructs a
4282: # structure to keep results.
4283: #
4284: 
4285: sub ex_ct_run
4286: {
4287:     my $self = shift;
4288: 
4289:     my $command_test = shift;
4290: 
4291:     my $execution_context = $self->{execution_context};
4292: 
4293:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
4294: 
4295:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
4296: 
4297:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
4298: 
4299:     # get the current module summary
4300: 
4301:     my $selected_module = $global_test_report->{selected}->{$module_name};
4302: 
4303:     # get the current test summary
4304: 
4305:     my $module_command_definitions = $selected_module->{command_definitions};
4306: 
4307:     my $command_definition_counter = $execution_context->get_command_definition_counter_per_module();
4308: 
4309:     my $selected_command_definition = $module_command_definitions->[$command_definition_counter - 1];
4310: 
4311:     # get the current command test
4312: 
4313:     if (not exists $selected_command_definition->{command_tests})
4314:     {
4315:         $selected_command_definition->{command_tests} = [];
4316:     }
4317: 
4318:     my $selected_test_definitions = $selected_command_definition->{command_tests};
4319: 
4320:     my $test_definition_counter = $execution_context->get_command_test_counter_per_command_definition();
4321: 
4322:     # if (not $selected_test_definitions->[$test_definition_counter])
4323:     # {
4324:     #   $selected_test_definitions->[$test_definition_counter] = {};
4325:     # }
4326: 
4327:     # just clone the current command test and add to the result
4328: 
4329:     use Clone 'clone';
4330: 
4331:     my $cloned_command_test = clone($command_test);
4332: 
4333:     # add the context_path
4334: 
4335:     my $context_path = $execution_context->get_context_path();;
4336: 
4337:     if ($option_verbose)
4338:     {
4339:         print $global_fd_output "$0: Preparing $context_path\n";
4340:     }
4341: 
4342:     $cloned_command_test->{context_path} = $context_path;
4343: 
4344:     push @$selected_test_definitions, $cloned_command_test;
4345: 
4346:     # my $selected_test_definition = $selected_test_definitions->[$test_definition_counter];
4347: 
4348:     # # add the current test description to the summary
4349: 
4350:     # $selected_test_definition->{description} = $command_test->{description};
4351: 
4352:     # always succeeds
4353: 
4354:     my $test_result
4355:         = {
4356:            after_match => "",
4357:            before_match => "",
4358:            error => "",
4359:            expected => "",
4360:            matched_pattern_position => "",
4361:            successfully_matching_string => "",
4362:           };
4363: 
4364:     return $test_result;
4365: }
4366: 
4367: 
4368: #
4369: # Adds the current command definition to the summary, constructs a
4370: # structure to keep results.
4371: #
4372: 
4373: sub ex_cd_start
4374: {
4375:     my $self = shift;
4376: 
4377:     # get the execution context
4378: 
4379:     my $execution_context = $self->{execution_context};
4380: 
4381:     # create an output entry for the module
4382: 
4383:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
4384: 
4385:     my $module_name = $module_definition->{name};
4386: 
4387:     # create an output entry for the current command definition
4388: 
4389:     my $selected_module = $global_test_report->{selected}->{$module_name};
4390: 
4391:     my $selected_command_definitions = $selected_module->{command_definitions};
4392: 
4393:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
4394: 
4395:     # just clone the current command_definition
4396: 
4397:     use Clone 'clone';
4398: 
4399: #     push @$selected_command_definitions, { description => $command_definition->{description}, tests => [], };
4400: 
4401:     my $cloned_command_definition = clone($command_definition);
4402: 
4403:     delete $cloned_command_definition->{command_tests};
4404: 
4405:     # add the context_path
4406: 
4407:     my $context_path = $execution_context->get_context_path();;
4408: 
4409:     if ($option_verbose)
4410:     {
4411:         print $global_fd_output "$0: Preparing $context_path\n";
4412:     }
4413: 
4414:     $cloned_command_definition->{context_path} = $context_path;
4415: 
4416:     # add to the result
4417: 
4418:     push @$selected_command_definitions, $cloned_command_definition;
4419: }
4420: 
4421: 
4422: sub ex_terminate
4423: {
4424:     my $self = shift;
4425: 
4426:     my $fd_output = shift;
4427: 
4428:     my $test_module_library_contents = shift;
4429: 
4430:     my $selected_output_levels = $Heterarch::Test::Element::selected_output_levels;
4431: 
4432:     my $extension = join '-', sort keys %$selected_output_levels;
4433: 
4434:     my $output_directory = "/tmp/test_report_$global_config->{package}->{name}";
4435: 
4436:     my $created;
4437: 
4438:     if ( !-d $output_directory )
4439:     {
4440:         use File::Path qw( make_path );
4441: 
4442:         $created = make_path($output_directory);
4443:     }
4444:     else
4445:     {
4446:         $created = "exists";
4447:     }
4448: 
4449:     if ($created)
4450:     {
4451:         my $output_filename = "test_report_$global_config->{package}->{name}_$extension";
4452: 
4453:         my $output_pathname = "$output_directory/$output_filename";
4454: 
4455:         $self->process($output_pathname, $test_module_library_contents, $global_test_report);
4456: 
4457:         # my $testing = 1;
4458: 
4459:         # if ($testing)
4460:         # {
4461:         #       my $output_filename = "/tmp/test_report_$global_config->{package}->{name}";
4462: 
4463:         #       $self->process($output_filename, $test_module_library_contents, $global_test_report);
4464:         # }
4465:     }
4466:     else
4467:     {
4468:         Heterarch::Test::Reporting::report_error_add
4469:                 (
4470:                  $fd_output,
4471:                  {
4472:                   description => "error creating the output_directory ($created)",
4473:                   error => "this test was tagged with the error flag",
4474:                   module_name => "END",
4475:                   subdescription => "error creating the output_directory ($created)",
4476:                  },
4477:                 );
4478:     }
4479: }
4480: 
4481: 
4482: package Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::HTMLTable;
4483: 
4484: # the Formatter:: HTMLTable package converts the test specifications
4485: # to HTML tables.
4486: 
4487: 
4488: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter");
4489: 
4490: 
4491: #
4492: # sub start
4493: #
4494: # Incomplete.
4495: #
4496: 
4497: sub ex_cd_start
4498: {
4499:     my $self = shift;
4500: 
4501:     my $execution_context = $self->{execution_context};
4502: 
4503:     my $module_definition = $execution_context->get_module_definition();
4504: 
4505:     my $command_definition = $execution_context->get_command_definition();
4506: 
4507:     my $fd_output = $self->{fd_output};
4508: 
4509:     # we have to either instantiate an object that produces output ...
4510: 
4511:     if ($command_definition->{class})
4512:     {
4513:         # instantiate the object
4514: 
4515:         my $class = $command_definition->{class};
4516: 
4517:         my $filename = $class . ".pm";
4518: 
4519:         $filename =~ s(::)(/)g;
4520: 
4521:         # require $filename;
4522: 
4523:         # $self->{command_object} = eval "$class->new()";
4524:     }
4525: 
4526:     # ... or run perl code that produces output
4527: 
4528:     elsif (ref $command_definition->{command} eq 'CODE')
4529:     {
4530:         # code is already instantiated, nothing to do here
4531:     }
4532: 
4533:     # ... or run a command that produces output
4534: 
4535:     else
4536:     {
4537:         # start the command and connect with its I/O channels
4538: 
4539:         # my ($exp, $test_startup) = $command_definition->spawn_new($fd_output);
4540: 
4541:         # remember to spawn a new command
4542: 
4543:         my $spawn_new = not $command_definition->{recycle};
4544: 
4545:         # retrieve the shell command and its arguments
4546: 
4547:         my ($command, $arguments) = $command_definition->parse_command_line();
4548: 
4549:         # if the command can be executed
4550: 
4551:         if (defined $command)
4552:         {
4553:             if ($spawn_new)
4554:             {
4555:             }
4556:             else
4557:             {
4558:                 # print $fd_output "*** Recycling $command " . (join ' ', map { "'$_'" } @$arguments) . "\n";
4559:             }
4560:         }
4561:     }
4562: }
4563: 
4564: 
4565: package Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Latex;
4566: 
4567: # The Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Latex package converts the test specifications
4568: # to a Latex document.
4569: 
4570: 
4571: our @ISA = ("Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter");
4572: 
4573: 
4574: our $output_file;
4575: 
4576: 
4577: sub _output_latex
4578: {
4579:     my $outputfile = shift;
4580: 
4581:     my $contents = shift;
4582: 
4583:     $contents =~ s/_/\\_/g;
4584:     $contents =~ s/&/\\&/g;
4585: 
4586:     print $outputfile $contents;
4587: }
4588: 
4589: 
4590: sub _output_latex_table
4591: {
4592:     my $outputfile = shift;
4593: 
4594:     my $contents = shift;
4595: 
4596:     my $prompt = shift;
4597: 
4598:     _output_latex_environment_prompt
4599:         (
4600:          $output_file,
4601:          {
4602:           content_type => "Verbatim",
4603:           prompt => $prompt,
4604:           suffix => "\n\n",
4605:          },
4606:         );
4607: 
4608:     _output_latex($output_file, '\begin{longtable}{p{13cm}}' . "\n");
4609: 
4610:     _output_latex($output_file, '\hline' . "\n");
4611: 
4612:     _output_latex_environment_prompt
4613:         (
4614:          $output_file,
4615:          {
4616:           content_type => "Verbatim",
4617:           content => $contents,
4618:          }
4619:         );
4620: 
4621:     _output_latex($output_file, '\\\\' . "\n");
4622: 
4623:     _output_latex($output_file, '\hline' . "\n");
4624: 
4625:     _output_latex($output_file, '\end{longtable}' . "\n");
4626: 
4627: }
4628: 
4629: 
4630: sub _output_latex_environment_prompt
4631: {
4632:     my $outputfile = shift;
4633: 
4634:     my $content_options = shift;
4635: 
4636:     my $content_line_limit = $content_options->{content_line_limit};
4637:     my $content_type = $content_options->{content_type};
4638:     my $content_type_options = $content_options->{content_type_options} || "";
4639:     my $content = $content_options->{content} || "";
4640:     my $prompt = $content_options->{prompt} || "";
4641:     my $suffix = $content_options->{suffix} || "";
4642: 
4643:     # if there is content
4644: 
4645:     if (defined $content)
4646:     {
4647:         # cut the content at the content limit
4648: 
4649:         if ($content_line_limit)
4650:         {
4651:             my $line_count = $content =~ tr/\n//;
4652: 
4653:             if ($line_count > $content_line_limit)
4654:             {
4655:                 my $first_lines = join "\n", ( split /\n/, $content )[0 .. $content_line_limit];
4656: 
4657:                 $content = $first_lines . "\n ... < cut at $content_line_limit lines, more follows > ...\n";
4658:             }
4659:         }
4660: 
4661:         # output the prompt
4662: 
4663:         _output_latex($output_file, $prompt);
4664: 
4665:         # output the content enclosed with the begin and end markers
4666: 
4667:         if ($content_type)
4668:         {
4669:             _output_latex($output_file, '\begin{' . $content_type . "}$content_type_options\n");
4670:         }
4671: 
4672:         _output_latex($output_file, $content);
4673: 
4674:         if ($content !~ /\n$/)
4675:         {
4676:             _output_latex($output_file, "\n");
4677:         }
4678: 
4679:         if ($content_type)
4680:         {
4681:             _output_latex($output_file, '\end{' . $content_type . "}\n");
4682:         }
4683:     }
4684: 
4685:     # output the suffix
4686: 
4687:     _output_latex($output_file, $suffix);
4688: }
4689: 
4690: 
4691: sub _output_latex_environment_minipage
4692: {
4693:     my $outputfile = shift;
4694: 
4695:     my $content_options = shift;
4696: 
4697:     my $content_line_limit = $content_options->{content_line_limit};
4698:     my $content_type = $content_options->{content_type};
4699:     my $content_type_embedding = $content_options->{content_type_embedding} || "";
4700:     my $content_type_options = $content_options->{content_type_options} || "";
4701:     my $content = $content_options->{content};
4702:     my $prompt = $content_options->{prompt} || "";
4703:     my $prompt_type = $content_options->{prompt_type};
4704:     my $suffix = $content_options->{suffix} || "";
4705: 
4706:     # if there is content
4707: 
4708:     if (defined $content)
4709:     {
4710:         # cut the content at the content limit
4711: 
4712:         if ($content_line_limit)
4713:         {
4714:             my $line_count = $content =~ tr/\n//;
4715: 
4716:             if ($line_count > $content_line_limit)
4717:             {
4718:                 my $first_lines = join "\n", ( split /\n/, $content )[0 .. $content_line_limit];
4719: 
4720:                 $content = $first_lines . "\n ... < cut at $content_line_limit lines, more follows > ...\n";
4721:             }
4722:         }
4723: 
4724:         # output the content begin marker
4725: 
4726:         if ($content_type)
4727:         {
4728:             print $output_file '\vspace{3ex}
4729: \begin{' . $content_type . "}$content_type_options\n";
4730:         }
4731: 
4732:         # output the embedding start
4733: 
4734:         if ($content_type_embedding)
4735:         {
4736:             print $outputfile "\\$content_type_embedding\{";
4737:         }
4738: 
4739:         # output the prompt
4740: 
4741:         _output_latex_prompt($output_file, $prompt_type, $prompt, "", "");
4742: 
4743:         # output the content
4744: 
4745:         _output_latex($output_file, $content);
4746: 
4747:         if ($content !~ /\n$/)
4748:         {
4749:             _output_latex($output_file, "\n");
4750:         }
4751: 
4752:         # output the embedding end
4753: 
4754:         if ($content_type_embedding)
4755:         {
4756:             print $outputfile "\}\n";
4757:         }
4758: 
4759:         # output the content end marker
4760: 
4761:         if ($content_type)
4762:         {
4763:             print $output_file '\end{' . $content_type . "}\n" . '\vspace{3ex}
4764: ';
4765:         }
4766:     }
4767: 
4768:     # output the suffix
4769: 
4770:     _output_latex($output_file, $suffix);
4771: }
4772: 
4773: 
4774: sub _output_latex_figure_generate_insert
4775: {
4776:     my $figure = shift;
4777: 
4778:     my $figure_class = exists $figure->{class} ? $figure->{class} : "simple";
4779: 
4780:     my $output_filename = shift;
4781: 
4782:     my $command_test = shift;
4783: 
4784:     my $figure_references = shift;
4785: 
4786:     my $read = shift;
4787: 
4788:     my $figure_class_2_package
4789:         = {
4790:            hh_gates_A_B => "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Figure::HHGatesAB",
4791:            hh_gates_alpha_beta => "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Figure::HHGatesAlphaBeta",
4792:            hh_gates_internal => "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Figure::HHGatesInternal",
4793:            hh_gates_steady_state_tau => "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Figure::HHGatesSteadyStateTau",
4794:            simple => "Heterarch::Test::Output::Formatter::Figure::Simple",
4795:           };
4796: 
4797:     my $figure_package = $figure_class_2_package->{$figure_class};
4798: 
4799:     # create the figure object, link it with the generated references
4800: 
4801:     my $figure_object = $figure_package->new($figure, $output_filename, $command_test, $figure_references, $read);
4802: 
4803:     # generate its reference
4804: 
4805:     my $label_error = $figure_object->generate_label('latex');
4806: 
4807:     # render the graph
4808: 
4809:     my $render_error = $figure_object->render();
4810: 
4811:     # insert the figure filename in the latex file
4812: 
4813:     my $insert_error = $figure_object->insert($figure);
4814: 
4815:     if ($label_error or $render_error or $insert_error)
4816:     {
4817:         #! note that this does not generate a label for referencing
4818: 
4819:         _output_latex_environment_minipage
4820:             (
4821:              $output_file,
4822:              {
4823:               content => "Please install GD::Graph to render figures",
4824:               content_type => "minipage",
4825:               content_type_options => '{14cm}',
4826:               content_type_embedding => "small",
4827:               prompt => "ERROR\\::",
4828:               prompt_type => 'textbf',
4829:               suffix => "\n\n",
4830:              },
4831:             );
4832:     }
4833: }
4834: 
4835: 
4836: sub _output_latex_figure_references_insert
4837: {
4838:     my $figure_references = shift;
4839: 
4840:     my $ss = shift;
4841: 
4842:     my $cd_1 = $ss->{cd_1};
4843: 
4844:     # my $fr_0 = $ss->{fr_0};
4845: 
4846:     # my $fr_1 = $ss->{fr_1};
4847: 
4848:     if ($cd_1)
4849:     {
4850:         print $output_file '\item [$\bullet$]';
4851:     }
4852: 
4853:     if (scalar @$figure_references eq 1)
4854:     {
4855:         print $output_file 'See figure~\ref{' . $figure_references->[0] . "}";
4856:     }
4857:     else
4858:     {
4859:         print $output_file 'See figures';
4860: 
4861:         while (my $figure_reference = shift @$figure_references)
4862:         {
4863:             if (scalar @$figure_references eq 0)
4864:             {
4865:                 print $output_file '~\ref{' . $figure_reference . '}';
4866:             }
4867:             elsif (scalar @$figure_references eq 1)
4868:             {
4869:                 print $output_file '~\ref{' . $figure_reference . '} and';
4870:             }
4871:             else
4872:             {
4873:                 print $output_file '~\ref{' . $figure_reference . '},';
4874:             }
4875:         }
4876:     }
4877: 
4878:     print $output_file ".\n\n";
4879: }
4880: 
4881: 
4882: sub _output_latex_module_comments
4883: {
4884:     my $module_definition = shift;
4885: 
4886:     _output_latex_environment_minipage
4887:         (
4888:          $output_file,
4889:          {
4890:           content => $module_definition->{comment},
4891:           content_type => "minipage",
4892:           content_type_options => '{14cm}',
4893:           content_type_embedding => "small",
4894:           prompt => "NOTE\\::",
4895:           prompt_type => 'textbf',
4896:           suffix => "\n\n",
4897:          },
4898:         );
4899: }
4900: 
4901: 
4902: sub _output_latex_module_documentation
4903: {
4904:     my $module_definition_documentation = shift;
4905: 
4906:     if ($module_definition_documentation)
4907:     {
4908:         _output_latex($output_file, "$module_definition_documentation->{purpose}\n\n");
4909: 
4910:         _output_latex($output_file, "$module_definition_documentation->{explanation}\n\n");
4911: 
4912:         # _output_latex($output_file, "Test commands follow.\n\n");
4913:     }
4914:     else
4915:     {
4916:         _output_latex($output_file, "\n\n");
4917:     }
4918: }
4919: 
4920: 
4921: sub _output_latex_module_filename_insert
4922: {
4923:     my $module_definition = shift;
4924: 
4925:     my $module_definition_documentation = shift;
4926: 
4927:     my $ss = shift;
4928: 
4929:     my $cd_0 = $ss->{cd_0};
4930: 
4931:     my $cd_1 = $ss->{cd_1};
4932: 
4933:     if ($cd_0)
4934:     {
4935:         _output_latex($output_file, 'File ');
4936: 
4937:         _output_latex_prompt($output_file, "textit", $module_definition->{name}, "", "");
4938: 
4939:         if ($module_definition_documentation)
4940:         {
4941:             _output_latex($output_file, ':  ');
4942:         }
4943:         else
4944:         {
4945:             _output_latex($output_file, '.');
4946:         }
4947:     }
4948:     elsif ($cd_1)
4949:     {
4950:         _output_latex($output_file, 'The file ');
4951: 
4952:         _output_latex_prompt($output_file, "textit", $module_definition->{name}, "", "");
4953: 
4954:         _output_latex($output_file, " defines the test that is explained in this section.\n\n");
4955:     }
4956:     else
4957:     {
4958:         _output_latex($output_file, 'The file ');
4959: 
4960:         _output_latex_prompt($output_file, "textit", $module_definition->{name}, "", "");
4961: 
4962:         _output_latex($output_file, " defines the tests that are explained in this section.\n\n");
4963:     }
4964: }
4965: 
4966: 
4967: sub _output_latex_module_harnessing
4968: {
4969:     my $module_definition = shift;
4970: 
4971:     my $ss = shift;
4972: 
4973:     my $cd_0 = $ss->{cd_0};
4974: 
4975:     if ($cd_0)
4976:     {
4977:         my $harnessing = $module_definition->{harnessing};
4978: 
4979:         if ($harnessing->{preparation})
4980:         {
4981:             _output_latex($output_file, '\begin{' . "itemize" . "}\n");
4982: 
4983:             _output_latex($output_file, '\item ');
4984: 
4985:             _output_latex_prompt($output_file, "textit", $module_definition->{harnessing}->{preparation}, "Preparation: ", "\n");
4986: 
4987:             _output_latex($output_file, '\item ');
4988: 
4989:             _output_latex_prompt($output_file, "textit", $module_definition->{harnessing}->{reparation}, "Reparation: ", "\n");
4990: 
4991:             _output_latex($output_file, '\end{' . "itemize" . "}\n");
4992:         }
4993:     }
4994: }
4995: 
4996: 
4997: sub _output_latex_postamble
4998: {
4999:     my $postamble
5000:         = '\end{document}
5001: 
5002: %%% Local Variables:
5003: %%% mode: latex
5004: %%% TeX-master: t
5005: %%% End:
5006: ';
5007: 
5008: #    $output_file = IO::File->new(">>$output_filename");
5009: 
5010:     print $output_file $postamble;
5011: }
5012: 
5013: 
5014: sub _output_latex_preamble
5015: {
5016:     my $output_filename = shift;
5017: 
5018:     my $html_introduction = shift;
5019: 
5020:     my $main_figures_directory = "$output_filename/figures";
5021: 
5022:     {
5023:         use File::Basename qw( fileparse );
5024:         use File::Path qw( make_path );
5025:         use File::Spec;
5026: 
5027:         my $created = make_path($main_figures_directory);
5028: 
5029:         use File::Copy;
5030: 
5031:         my $logo_source_filename = "$ENV{HOME}/projects/neurocampus/source/snapshots/master/invoices/inv-draft-only/figures/ns-main-logo-dark2.jpg";
5032: 
5033:         copy($logo_source_filename, "$main_figures_directory")
5034:             or die "Copy of $logo_source_filename failed: $!";
5035:     }
5036: 
5037:     $output_file = IO::File->new(">$output_filename.tex");
5038: 
5039:     my $title_paragraphs = _output_latex_preamble_construct($html_introduction);
5040: 
5041:     my $preamble1
5042:         = '\documentclass[12pt,a4paper]{article}
5043: \usepackage[a4paper, total={6in, 9in}]{geometry}
5044: \usepackage{amsfonts}
5045: \usepackage{amsmath}
5046: \usepackage{amsthm}
5047: \usepackage{color}
5048: \usepackage[colorlinks=true]{hyperref}
5049: % \usepackage[dvips]{epsfig}
5050: \usepackage{enumitem} % [noitemsep,nolistsep] compact itemize/enumerate etc.
5051: \usepackage{graphicx}
5052: \usepackage{longtable}
5053: % \usepackage[margin=2cm]{geometry}
5054: \usepackage{subcaption}
5055: \usepackage{url}
5056: \usepackage{verbatim}
5057: \usepackage{fancyvrb}
5058: \usepackage{relsize}
5059: 
5060: \usepackage{footmisc}   % ADC: Added footnote referencing capacity for text editing comments
5061: 
5062: % Typeface
5063: \usepackage[condensed,math]{iwona}
5064: \usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
5065: 
5066: \setlength{\textwidth}{15.7cm}
5067: \setlength{\textheight}{22cm}
5068: 
5069: \setlength{\oddsidemargin}{0pt}
5070: 
5071: \setlength{\topmargin}{0cm}
5072: \setlength{\headheight}{1cm}
5073: \setlength{\headsep}{0.5cm}
5074: \setlength{\topskip}{0cm}
5075: 
5076: ';
5077: 
5078:     print $output_file $preamble1;
5079: 
5080:     my $use_title_page = "";
5081: 
5082:     if ($use_title_page)
5083:     {
5084:         _output_latex_preamble_configure_title_page($title_paragraphs);
5085:     }
5086: 
5087:     my $preamble2
5088:         = '
5089: \begin{document}
5090: 
5091: \begin{center}
5092:    \includegraphics[scale=0.5]{' . $main_figures_directory . '/ns-main-logo-dark2.jpg}
5093: %   \caption{{\bf A Dummy Figure:} Example of \LaTeX\,\,\,code to incorporate a figure into documentation.}
5094:   \label{fig:logo}
5095: \end{center}
5096: \vspace{-10ex}
5097: 
5098: ';
5099: 
5100:     if ($use_title_page)
5101:     {
5102:         $preamble2 .= '
5103:        \maketitle
5104: 
5105: ';
5106:     }
5107: 
5108:     print $output_file $preamble2;
5109: 
5110:     if (not $use_title_page)
5111:     {
5112:         _output_latex($output_file, '\section*{\center \Huge ' . $title_paragraphs->{title} . "}\n\n");
5113: 
5114:         _output_latex($output_file, '\vspace{-6ex}' . "\n\n");
5115: 
5116:         _output_latex($output_file, '\section*{\center \scriptsize ' . "Automatically generated draft document" . "}\n\n");
5117: 
5118:         _output_latex($output_file, '\vspace{-6ex}' . "\n\n");
5119: 
5120:         _output_latex($output_file, '\section*{\center \small \textbf ' . $title_paragraphs->{author} . "}\n\n");
5121: 
5122:         _output_latex($output_file, '\vspace{-6ex}' . "\n\n");
5123: 
5124:         use POSIX qw(strftime);
5125: 
5126:         # my $now_string = strftime("%a %b %e %H:%M:%S %Y", localtime());
5127: 
5128:         my $now_string = strftime("%a %b %e, %Y", localtime());
5129: 
5130:         my ( $day, $month_number, $year ) = ( localtime ) [3,4,5];
5131: 
5132:         $year += 1900;
5133: 
5134:         my $month = (
5135:                      qw/ January   February  March
5136:                          April     May       June
5137:                          July      August    September
5138:                          October   November  December  /
5139:                     ) [$month_number];
5140: 
5141:         $now_string = "$day $month, $year";
5142: 
5143:         _output_latex($output_file, '\section*{\center \small \textbf ' . $now_string . "}\n\n");
5144:     }
5145: 
5146:     _output_latex_preamble_abstract($title_paragraphs);
5147: 
5148:     _output_latex_preamble_summary($title_paragraphs);
5149: }
5150: 
5151: 
5152: sub _output_latex_preamble_abstract
5153: {
5154:     my $title_paragraphs = shift;
5155: 
5156: }
5157: 
5158: 
5159: sub _output_latex_preamble_construct
5160: {
5161:     my $html_introduction_original = shift;
5162: 
5163:     my $html_introduction = $html_introduction_original;
5164: 
5165:     # extract email address
5166: 
5167:     $html_introduction =~ m(<address>(.*)</address>)gs;
5168: 
5169:     my $email_anchored = $1;
5170: 
5171:     my $author = $email_anchored || "";
5172: 
5173:     $author =~ m(<a.*?>(.*)</a>)gs;
5174: 
5175:     $author = $1 || "";
5176: 
5177:     my $thanks = $email_anchored || "";
5178: 
5179:     $thanks =~ m("mailto:(.*)")gs;
5180: 
5181:     $thanks = $1 || "";
5182: 
5183:     # remove trailing part
5184: 
5185:     $html_introduction =~ s(<address>(.*))()gs;
5186: 
5187:     # extract title
5188: 
5189:     $html_introduction =~ s(<h1>(.*)</h1>)()gs;
5190: 
5191:     my $title = $1 || "";
5192: 
5193:     # remove irrelevant html tags
5194: 
5195:     $html_introduction =~ s(<p/?>)()gs;
5196: 
5197:     $html_introduction =~ s(<hr/?>)()gs;
5198: 
5199:     # split in paragraphs
5200: 
5201:     my $paragraphs = [ split "\n\n", $html_introduction, ];
5202: 
5203:     # construct and return result
5204: 
5205:     my $result
5206:         = {
5207:            author => $author,
5208:            email_anchored => $email_anchored,
5209:            original_contents => $html_introduction_original,
5210:            paragraphs => $paragraphs,
5211:            thanks => $thanks,
5212:            title => $title,
5213:           };
5214: 
5215:     return $result
5216: }
5217: 
5218: 
5219: sub _output_latex_preamble_summary
5220: {
5221:     my $title_paragraphs = shift;
5222: 
5223:     my $paragraphs = $title_paragraphs->{paragraphs};
5224: 
5225:     if (scalar @$paragraphs)
5226:     {
5227:         # output the description as a section title
5228: 
5229:         _output_latex($output_file, '\section*{\center ' . "Summary" . "}\n\n");
5230: 
5231:         # write paragraphs
5232: 
5233:         foreach my $paragraph (@$paragraphs)
5234:         {
5235:             _output_latex($output_file, "$paragraph\n\n");
5236:         }
5237:     }
5238: }
5239: 
5240: 
5241: sub _output_latex_preamble_configure_title_page
5242: {
5243:     my $title_paragraphs = shift;
5244: 
5245:     # write title
5246: 
5247:     _output_latex($output_file, '\title{' . $title_paragraphs->{title} . "}\n\n");
5248: 
5249:     _output_latex($output_file, '\date{' . localtime() . "}\n\n");
5250: 
5251:     _output_latex($output_file, '\author{' . $title_paragraphs->{author} . "}\n\n");
5252: 
5253: }
5254: 
5255: 
5256: sub _output_latex_command_definition_command_arguments
5257: {
5258:     my $command_definition = shift;
5259: 
5260:     my $ss = shift;
5261: 
5262:     my $cd_1 = $ss->{cd_1};
5263: 
5264:     my $ct_1 = $ss->{ct_1};
5265: 
5266:     # retrieve the shell command and its arguments
5267: 
5268:     my ($command, $arguments) = $command_definition->parse_command_line();
5269: 
5270:     my $command_string = join ' ', $command, @$arguments;
5271: 
5272:     if ($cd_1)
5273:     {
5274:         print $output_file '\item [$\bullet$]';
5275:     }
5276: 
5277:     if ($ct_1)
5278:     {
5279:         _output_latex($output_file, 'The test is started with system shell command: ');
5280:     }
5281:     else
5282:     {
5283:         _output_latex($output_file, 'The tests are started with the system shell command: ');
5284:     }
5285: 
5286:     _output_latex_prompt($output_file, "textbf", $command_string, "", "\n\n");
5287: }
5288: 
5289: 
5290: sub _output_latex_command_definition_comments
5291: {
5292:     my $command_definition = shift;
5293: 
5294:     my $ss = shift;
5295: 
5296:     my $cd_1_comment = $ss->{cd_1_comment};
5297: 
5298:     if ($cd_1_comment)
5299:     {
5300:         print $output_file '\item [$\bullet$]';
5301: 
5302:         _output_latex_environment_minipage
5303:             (
5304:              $output_file,
5305:              {
5306:               content => $command_definition->{comment},
5307:               content_type => "minipage",
5308:               content_type_options => '{14cm}',
5309:               content_type_embedding => "small",
5310:               prompt => "NOTE\\::",
5311:               prompt_type => 'textbf',
5312:               suffix => "\n\n",
5313:              },
5314:             );
5315:     }
5316: }
5317: 
5318: 
5319: sub _output_latex_command_definition_description
5320: {
5321:     my $command_definition = shift;
5322: 
5323:     my $ss = shift;
5324: 
5325:     my $cd_1 = $ss->{cd_1};
5326: 
5327:     my $ct_1 = $ss->{ct_1};
5328: 
5329:     my $description = $command_definition->{description};
5330: 
5331:     my $command_tests = $command_definition->{command_tests};
5332: 
5333:     if ($cd_1)
5334:     {
5335:         print $output_file '\item [$\bullet$]';
5336:
